speaker: DAD
text: It was adventurous... The land below is alive with FANTASY CREATURES. We pass through a field of racing CENTAURS.

speaker: DAD
text: Exciting... Mermaids leap through shimming water and FAIRIES fly through the air, spiraling up into the sky.

speaker: DAD
text: And best of all... there was MAGIC! We stop on an ELF WIZARD holding a gnarled wooden staff. He turns and thrusts his staff toward the sky, casting a spell.

speaker: ELF WIZARD
text: Boombastia! Fireworks shoot magically into the air. Fantasy creatures across the land watch in awe.

speaker: DAD
text: And that magic helped all in need. A SATYR WIZARD approaches.

speaker: SATYR WIZARD
text: Flame Infernar! A fire appears and they happily warm themselves by it.

speaker: BRAVE WIZARD
text: Voltar Thundasir! A lighting bolt zaps the ceiling, crashing rocks down in front of the dragon, clearing their path to the chalice.

speaker: DAD
text: But it wasn’t easy to master. And so the world found a simpler way to get by... The wizard and apprentice see a satyr INVENTOR in a barn surrounded by fantasy creatures.

speaker: APPRENTICE
text: ‘Tis so easy! The wizard looks on, disappointed.

speaker: MASTER WIZARD
text: Huh. An elf on a sales floor lights up a gas stove with a click, click. Everyone reacts in amazement. Goblins sit in a cozy living room. One of them turns on a gas fireplace using a remote.

speaker: DAD
text: Over time, magic faded away... A centaur plays “Prance Prance Revolution” in an arcade. A mermaid lounges in a kiddie pool, scrolling on her phone. A sprite, suitcase in hand, approaches an awaiting airplane. A factory assembly line rolls by. Traffic crawls along a congested highway.

speaker: DAD
text: But I hope there’s a little magic left... A phone alarm beeps. A boy’s hand quickly silences it. Puts on his watch with style. His shoes with flair.

speaker: DAD
text: ...in you. He pulls on his sweatshirt and gets stuck. He pulls harder until his big nose, wild hair, and giant ears pop out of the sweatshirt, revealing a lanky teenage elf: IAN LIGHTFOOT. Ian smiles and dusts off the “Willowdale College” sweatshirt. He grabs his backpack and pauses to look at his desk calendar. Today is circled and reads: 16TH BDAY. Ian looks into the mirror, smiles, and walks out of the room.

speaker: FITNESS INSTRUCTOR
text: We’re gonna get Warrior Z90 fit! Let me hear you say I’m a mighty warrior!

speaker: FITNESS MODELS
text: I’m a mighty warrior!

speaker: LAUREL
text: I’m a mighty warrior!

speaker: IAN
text: Morning, Mom! A scaly Labrador-sized dragon, BLAZEY, races into the room and tackles Ian to the ground.

speaker: IAN
text: Aahh! Ian is trapped as the dragon happily licks his face.

speaker: IAN
text: Shh! Down girl! Please!

speaker: LAUREL
text: Oh--! Blazey, down! Laurel races over, squirting the dragon with a spray bottle.

speaker: IAN
text: Ah! Stop, girl, stop!

speaker: LAUREL
text: Bad dragon! Back to your lair! Blazey blows a small puff of fire at Laurel and runs off. Ian stands and is attacked with Mom kisses.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Happy Birthday, mister adult man!

speaker: IAN
text: Ugh, no, Mom! Ah! gross.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Hey, buddy, don’t wipe off my kisses! Laurel looks at Ian, touched.

speaker: IAN
text: What?

speaker: LAUREL
text: You’re wearing your Dad’s sweatshirt.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh, you know. Finally fits.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Oh, my little chubby cheeks is all grown up!

speaker: IAN
text: Okay, okay, Mom! I gotta eat something before school.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Ugh, we don’t have much food. I still have to go to the grocery store-- Laurel smacks Ian’s hand as he reaches for something.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Hands off, Mister! Those are for your party tonight. Ian takes the milk from the fridge and closes the door.

speaker: IAN
text: It’s not a party Mom, it just us. Ian empties the last of a box of cereal into a bowl.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Well, you could invite those kids from your science class. You said they seemed pretty “rockin’.”

speaker: IAN
text: I’m pretty sure I didn’t say it like that. And besides, I don’t even know them.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Well, your birthday is a day to try new things, be the “new you”! Laurel makes herself a smoothie, yelling over the blender.

speaker: LAUREL
text: SPEAKING OF TRYING NEW THINGS, DID YOU SIGN UP FOR DRIVING PRACTICE?! The blender stops, but Ian is still shouting.

speaker: IAN
text: NO! No. Laurel pours her shake into a glass.

speaker: LAUREL
text: I know you’re a little scared to drive sweetie-pie, but--

speaker: IAN
text: I’m not scared, Mom. I’m gonna move Barley’s game.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Okay, but you know how he gets when someone touches that board. Ian walks to the kitchen table, stumbling over a plastic flail on the floor. The table is covered with a game: pewter figures, dice, and miniatures. Ian and begins to move them--

speaker: IAN
text: Well, he’s gotta learn how to clean up his toys-- --when suddenly a huge, thick arm wraps around his neck.

speaker: BARLEY
text: HALT!

speaker: IAN
text: AHH! Ian is pulled into the hulking barrel chest of twenty-yearold BARLEY LIGHTFOOT.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Doth my brother dare disrupt an active campaign?! Ian’s cereal bowl is knocked to the floor in the scuffle.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh, come on!

speaker: BARLEY
text: You know, Ian, in the days of old, a boy of sixteen would have his strength tested in the Swamps of Despair.

speaker: IAN
text: I’m not testing anything, just let me go!

speaker: LAUREL
text: Let him go. Barley releases Ian.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Okay, but I know you're stronger than that. There’s a mighty warrior inside you. You just have to let him out. Riiight, Mom? Barley puts Laurel in the same chokehold as she laughs.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh! That’s good! Keepin’ your head down.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Barley, you stink! When was the last time you showered? Blazey roars and leaps around. Amidst the chaos, Ian picks up his bowl from the floor and retreats to the kitchen counter.

speaker: BARLEY
text: If you tried a little harder, you actually could probably wiggle out of this-- Suddenly, Laurel flips Barley onto his back with a CRASH.

speaker: BARLEY
text: See? Mom knows how to let out her inner warrior. Barley reaches up to high-five Laurel.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Thank you. Now take out the trash.

speaker: OFFICER GORE
text: --stay on the lookout for a runaway Griffin.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Officer Bronco. OFFICER COLT BRONCO, a mustachioed, cocky cop with mirrored aviator sunglasses, speaks over the clop of horse hooves.

speaker: COLT
text: Barley, Barley, Barley. Every time the city tries to tear down an old piece of rubble, I gotta drag my rear end out here and deal with you. As he steps closer, we see he’s not a mounted police officer after all. He’s a centaur cop.

speaker: BARLEY
text: I don’t know what you’re talking about. Laurel appears in the doorway behind Barley, irritated.

speaker: COLT
text: Oh, really? Colt pulls out his phone. A video shows Barley chained to an ancient fountain in front of a laughing crowd of people.

speaker: BARLEY
text: I will not let you tear this down this fountain! Ancient warriors on grand quests drank from it’s flowing waters! As construction workers pull him away, Barley falls into the water with a SPLASH.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Barley.

speaker: BARLEY
text: They’re destroying the town’s past!

speaker: COLT
text: And last night, someone destroyed their bulldozer.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Ugh, well come on in, rest your haunches for a minute.

speaker: COLT
text: Thank you, hon. Laurel and Colt kiss. Ian and Barley shudder in disgust.

speaker: COLT
text: Hey there, Birthday Boy... So ya workin’ hard or hardly workin’? Colt punches Ian on the shoulder just a little too hard.

speaker: IAN
text: I'm just, you know... making a toast.

speaker: LAUREL
text: I’m serious, Barley, you need to start thinking less about the past and more about your future.

speaker: COLT
text: She’s right. As Colt turns, his tail knocks Ian’s toast off the counter. It falls to the floor and is promptly snatched up by Blazey.

speaker: COLT
text: You can’t spend all day playing your board game. Colt awkwardly plops his hindquarters into a chair at the table, toppling Quests of Yore pieces. Barley hustles over to set the pieces straight.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Uh, Quests of Yore isn’t just a board game, it’s an historicallybased role playing scenario. Did you know in the old days centaurs could run seventy miles an hour?

speaker: COLT
text: I own a vehicle, don’t need to run.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Well, Ian, you could definitely learn a lot from Quests of Yore. You want to play?

speaker: IAN
text: I don’t. Ian folds the last piece of toast and starts to take a bite--

speaker: BARLEY
text: You could be a crafty rogue, or... ooh, I know! You can be a wizard. Barley puts the pewter wizard figure on Ian’s shoulder.

speaker: BARLEY
text: I shall cast a spell on thee!!

speaker: IAN
text: Hey, careful of Dad’s sweatshirt.

speaker: BARLEY
text: I don’t even remember Dad wearing that sweatshirt.

speaker: IAN
text: Well, you do only have, like, two memories of him.

speaker: BARLEY
text: No! I've got three. I remember his beard was scratchy, he had a goofy laugh, and I used to play--

speaker: BARLEY
text: --drums on his feet.

speaker: IAN
text: --drums on his feet. Right.

speaker: BARLEY
text: I used to go... Barley hums “Shave and a Haircut” with raspberries for the last two beat, but clumsily knocks Ian’s toast off the counter. Ian lunges for it, but Barley gets it first.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Whoops! Five second rule. As Barley puts the toast back on the plate, his wrist cuff snags on Ian’s sweatshirt, ripping it. Ian GASPS.

speaker: BARLEY
text: It's okay! You just gotta pull it! Barley yanks his arm to snap the snagged thread, but only rips the seam even more.

speaker: IAN
text: NO!

speaker: LAUREL
text: Barley! Laurel cuts the thread. Ian walks away, trying to stay calm.

speaker: IAN
text: You know what, I’m just gonna get some food on the way to school.

speaker: LAUREL
text: I’ll sew that later tonight, okay? Barley jumps up, puts on a helmet, and grabs a sword.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Wait, wait, wait, wait! By the Laws of Yore, I must dub thee a man today! Kneel before me.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh, that’s okay... I gotta get going!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Alright, well I’ll pick you up later and we’ll perform the ceremony at school!

speaker: IAN
text: NO! No, no, no, no, no. Don’t do that. Okay, bye! Ian closes the door behind him and lets out a massive SIGH.

speaker: GAXTON
text: Hey! Go Griffins!

speaker: IAN
text: What? Gaxton points to Ian’s hoodie.

speaker: GAXTON
text: You go to Willowdale College? Gaxton’s son goes to the counter to wait for their food.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh, no. This was my Dad’s. Ian points to the name LIGHTFOOT on the hoodie.

speaker: GAXTON
text: “Lightfoot?” Wilden Lightfoot? Ian lights up.

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah!

speaker: GAXTON
text: You’re kidding! I went to college with him!

speaker: IAN
text: Really?

speaker: GAXTON
text: Yeah! Boy, I was so sorry to hear that he passed away. Ian nods and smiles.

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah... thanks.

speaker: GAXTON
text: You know, your dad was a great guy! So confident. When he came into a room, people noticed. The man wore the ugliest purple socks, every single day.

speaker: IAN
text: What? Why?

speaker: GAXTON
text: Hey, that’s exactly what we asked. But he was just bold. I always wished I had a little bit of that in me.

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah. Wow. I’ve never heard any of this about him before. Do you remember--? GAXTON'S SON calls out, waving a bag of food.

speaker: GAXTON'S SON
text: Dad!

speaker: GAXTON
text: Oh! Sorry. Gotta get this guy off to school. Gaxton holds out his hand to shake Ian’s.

speaker: GAXTON
text: Hey, it was nice meeting you!

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah, you too. Ian thinks to himself for a beat.

speaker: IAN
text: Huh. Bold.

speaker: TEACHER
text: Okay, class, sit down. We’re starting roll. GORGAMON, a slobby, flip-flop-wearing troll, has his feet on Ian’s chair.

speaker: IAN
text: Hey, uh, Gorgamon... um, would you mind not putting your feet on my chair today?

speaker: GORGAMON
text: Sorry dude, gotta keep ’em elevated. Gets the blood flowing to my brain.

speaker: IAN
text: It just makes it a little hard for me to fit in there--

speaker: GORGAMON
text: Well if I don’t have good blood flow, I can’t concentrate on my school work. You don’t want me to do bad school, do you?

speaker: IAN
text: Uh... no.

speaker: GORGAMON
text: Thanks, bro. Ian sits, scooting up to make room for the gross feet.

speaker: DRIVING INSTRUCTOR
text: First road test, any volunteers? A single hand raises from the back of a group of students. The crowd parts to reveal a nervous Ian.

speaker: DRIVING INSTRUCTOR
text: A left here. Ian signals and turns.

speaker: DRIVING INSTRUCTOR
text: Now take this on-ramp to the freeway.

speaker: IAN
text: Okay, yeah. I’m super ready for that. Ian’s smile fades as he nears the ZOOMING freeway traffic.

speaker: IAN
text: It’s nice and fast.

speaker: DRIVING INSTRUCTOR
text: Just merge into traffic.

speaker: IAN
text: Yup, just any minute... He grows more nervous.

speaker: DRIVING INSTRUCTOR
text: Merge into traffic!

speaker: IAN
text: Uh...

speaker: DRIVING INSTRUCTOR
text: MERGE INTO TRAFFIC! Panicked, Ian tries to merge. A car lays on its horn and Ian retreats back into his lane.

speaker: IAN
text: I’m not ready!

speaker: DRIVING INSTRUCTOR
text: Pull over. Ian pulls the car off the freeway.

speaker: SADALIA
text: Alright, so what should we do this weekend?

speaker: GURGE
text: Move to a cooler town? We see Ian nearby, alone, rehearsing some lines to himself.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh, hey! What’s up, dudes? I’m, uh, having a party tonight, and was wondering if you wanted to come over and get down on some cake... Ian's smile drops. Ian checks the writing on his hand.

speaker: IAN
text: That’s not something anyone says. Okay, don't say dudes. ...Gang? "What's up, gang?" He crosses out “dudes” and changes it to “gang.”

speaker: IAN
text: What’s up, gang?! Ian nods, then nervously heads over to the TEENS.

speaker: IAN
text: Hey, what’s up, gang? The teens turn to Ian.

speaker: SADALIA
text: Oh, hey... Ian, right?

speaker: IAN
text: Oh, I didn’t know you knew my... Ian looks at his hand. The writing is smeared and sweaty.

speaker: IAN
text: Uhhh, anyway... if you like parties, then I was gonna do a party.

speaker: SADALIA
text: What?

speaker: IAN
text: What I was trying to say, is if you’re not doing anything tonight-- but I’m sure you probably are doing something tonight--and you like cake like I like cake, I’ve got a cake... at my house. The teens look at each other.

speaker: SADALIA
text: Are you inviting us to a party?

speaker: IAN
text: That’s the one.

speaker: SADALIA
text: Oh, yeah! We don’t have any plans. The others all agree. Ian looks up, surprised.

speaker: IAN
text: Really? I guess we can just take the bus over to my house-- Ian is suddenly distracted by metal music in the near distance, a hard-working engine, a backfire. The teens look over as a disaster of a van pulls up, its side painted with a rearing Pegasus surrounded by lightning bolts.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh, no, no, no, no, no. It’s Barley. He spots Ian and sticks his head out the window.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ha-ha! Is that the birthday boy I see?! Ian cringes. The van swerves into the school pickup area, and comes to a stop halfway up onto the curb. Barley steps out, in full fantasy garb, and gestures grandly.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Behold! Your chariot awaits! The group looks at Ian. Ian keeps his back turned to the van.

speaker: ALTHEA
text: Do you know that guy?

speaker: IAN
text: Uh...

speaker: BARLEY
text: Sir Iandore of Lightfoot!

speaker: ALTHEA
text: Seems like he’s talking to you.

speaker: BARLEY
text: HEY, IAN! Barley honks the horn. Ian turns around sheepishly.

speaker: IAN
text: Hey, Barley... Yeah, we’re actually gonna take the bus...

speaker: BARLEY
text: The bus?! Nay! I will give you and your companions transport upon Guinevere!

speaker: PARTHENOPE
text: Um, who’s Guinevere?

speaker: BARLEY
text: My mighty steed! Barley slaps the hood of the van. The bumper falls off.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oops, that’s embarrassing! That’s okay, girl. We’ll patch you back up. Ian puts his hand to his face, embarrassed.

speaker: IAN
text: Ugh... He’s just joking around. Ian removes his hand to reveal the ink from his palm is now on his face.

speaker: SADALIA
text: You’ve got something on your face. Ian wipes his cheek, smearing ink there.

speaker: SADALIA
text: Oh no, you just--oh! Right. Ian wipes the other side spreading more ink around.

speaker: SADALIA
text: No, it’s--nope. Ian looks at his hand, then panics.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh! Uh... You know what? I just remembered that my birthday is, uh... cancelled.

speaker: ALTHEA
text: What?

speaker: IAN
text: I mean, the party. It was never actually happening. It was just this huge misunderstanding, so I gotta go. Okay, Bye! Ian races into Barley’s van.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ooh, whoops! Sorry! Let me just file those. Barley shoves the parking tickets into the glovebox, then notices the smeared letters on Ian’s forehead.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Hey! Did those kids write on your face? Barley licks his thumb and starts to rub Ian’s forehead.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Here, I’ll get it. Ian pushes Barley’s hand away.

speaker: IAN
text: Can we please just go home?

speaker: BARLEY
text: Okay, we'll perform your birthday ceremony later. Then you’ll be ready for adulthood and it’s gauntlet of challenges! You know in ancient times, you celebrated your day of birth with a solemn quest. Ian looks at the cartoon dragon on the side of the gym, giving an exaggerated thumbs up. Ian slumps in his seat.

speaker: IAN
text: Mom? No answer.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Will, you’re not gonna get that thing working.

speaker: DAD
text: I think I’ve got it.

speaker: LAUREL
text: I’m gonna watch from over here when it blows up. Laurel moves out of range of the microphone. We only hear Dad, then pauses for Laurel’s distant, indecipherable words.

speaker: DAD
text: Hello? Hello?

speaker: LAUREL
text: I’ll bet good money you can’t get it to work.

speaker: DAD
text: Oh, is that right?

speaker: LAUREL
text: Yup. But you’re doing a good job of making it look like you know what you’re doing.

speaker: DAD
text: Well, I’m trying to.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Did you check if it had batteries?

speaker: LAUREL
text: No, I’m serious.

speaker: DAD
text: I know.

speaker: LAUREL
text: So, is it really working?

speaker: DAD
text: Well, let’s find out. Okay. Bye. Ian smiles, rewinds the tape, and plays it again.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Will, you’re not gonna get that thing working.

speaker: DAD
text: I think I’ve got it.

speaker: LAUREL
text: I’m gonna watch from over here when it blows up.

speaker: IAN
text: Hi, Dad. We see close-ups of the pictures on the bulletin board as Ian begins to talk over the tape, forming a conversation.

speaker: DAD
text: Hello? Hello?

speaker: IAN
text: It’s me, Ian.

speaker: DAD
text: Oh, is that right?

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah. Did you have a good day?

speaker: DAD
text: Well, I’m trying to.

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah, me too. Although, I could clearly use some help.

speaker: IAN
text: I sure do wish I could spend a day with you sometime.

speaker: DAD
text: I know.

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah, there’s so many things we could do, I bet it’d be really fun!

speaker: DAD
text: Well, let’s find out.

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah, I mean, I’d love to! We could, uh--

speaker: DAD
text: Okay. Bye.

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah... Bye. The tape stops. Ian sits back, deflated.

speaker: LAUREL
text: AH! MY--! She opens the basement door and calls to Barley over the din of his music.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Barley, keep your soldiers off my land or our kingdoms will go to war!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Sorry, Mom!

speaker: LAUREL
text: Ugh, this is the world’s longest gap year. Laurel sees Ian on the couch, sewing his sweater.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Honey, I was gonna do that.

speaker: IAN
text: That’s okay. Laurel watches him.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Wow, you must have been taught by some kind of sewing master.

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah, a very humble sewing master. Ian finishes sewing. He holds out the thread and Laurel cuts it with the scissors. They’ve clearly done this a lot. Ian rubs his fingers over the word stitched on the breast of the hoodie: “LIGHTFOOT.”

speaker: IAN
text: What was Dad like when he was my age? Was he always super confident?

speaker: LAUREL
text: Oh, no. It took him a while to find out who he was.

speaker: IAN
text: I wish I’d met him.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Oh, me too. But hey, you know, when your dad got sick, he fought so hard... because he wanted to meet you more than anything. Laurel watches Ian, heartbroken.

speaker: LAUREL
text: You know what, I have something for you. I was going to wait until after cake but... I think you’ve waited long enough.

speaker: IAN
text: What is it?

speaker: LAUREL
text: It’s a gift... from your dad.

speaker: BARLEY
text: What do you mean it’s from Dad?

speaker: IAN
text: I don’t know! Mom said it was for both of us.

speaker: BARLEY
text: What is it?! Laurel descends the ladder with a long, canvas-wrapped item.

speaker: LAUREL
text: He just said to give you this when you were both over sixteen. I have no idea what it is.

speaker: BARLEY
text: NO WAY! Barley picks it up in amazement.

speaker: BARLEY
text: It’s a wizard staff! DAD WAS A WIZARD!

speaker: IAN
text: What?

speaker: LAUREL
text: Hold on, your Dad was an accountant. I mean, he got interested in a lot of strange things when he got sick but-- Laurel and Ian are confused. Ian looks back under the cloth and sees a rolled up piece of parchment.

speaker: IAN
text: There’s a letter. “Dear Ian and Barley: Long ago, the world was full of wonder! It was adventurous, exciting, and best of all... there was magic. And that magic helped all in need. But it wasn’t easy to master. And so the world found a simpler way to get by. Over time, magic faded away, but I hope there’s a little magic left... in you. And so I wrote this spell so I could see for myself, who my boys grew up to be.” Ian looks at the second page of the letter.

speaker: IAN
text: Visitation Spell? Barley takes the page from Ian and examines it. It reads VISITATION SPELL and has a diagram of a wizard staff with a beam of light shooting from it, forming a person.

speaker: BARLEY
text: I don't believe this. This spell brings him back. For one whole day, Dad will be back!

speaker: LAUREL
text: What?

speaker: IAN
text: Back?! Like back to life? That’s not possible.

speaker: BARLEY
text: It is with this.

speaker: IAN
text: I’m gonna meet Dad? Laurel grabs the letter from the boys. She’s beside herself.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Oh, Will, you wonderful nut, what is this?! Barley digs through the cloth.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Now, a spell this powerful needs an assist element. For this to work Dad would’ve had to find a... Phoenix Gem! Barley pulls out an orange gem and exams it.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Wow. There’s only a few of these left.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Hold on, is this dangerous?

speaker: BARLEY
text: We’re about to find out. Barley puts the gem in the staff, holds out the staff, and gets suddenly serious. Everyone waits in suspense.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ah!

speaker: LAUREL
text: What?!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Splinter. Barley grabs the staff, strikes a pose in the center of the room, and begins to read the spell.

speaker: BARLEY
text: "Only once is all we get, grant me this rebirth. 'Til tomorrow's sun has set, one day to walk the earth!” But nothing happens.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Hold on, I was just gripping it wrong. Barley adjusts his hands.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Only once is all we get, grant me this rebirth, ‘til tomorrow’s sun has set, one day to walk THE EARTH! Still nothing. In quick cuts, Barley tries again and again, with no success.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Only once is all we get, grant me this rebirth. He tries again.

speaker: BARLEY
text: ‘Til tomorrow’s sun has set... And again.

speaker: BARLEY
text: ...one day to walk THE EARTH! And again, exhausted.

speaker: BARLEY
text: One day to walk the earth! Losing his patience.

speaker: BARLEY
text: ‘Til tomorrow’s sun has set... Again, frustrated.

speaker: BARLEY
text: One day to walk the earth! With everything he has left.

speaker: BARLEY
text: ...One day... to walk... the earth! One more big attempt.

speaker: BARLEY
text: ‘Til tomorrow’s sun has set... Laurel and Ian have already given up.

speaker: BARLEY
text: One day to walk the earth!

speaker: LAUREL
text: Barley. He looks back to her, then puts the staff down and sits on the bed. Laurel puts her hand on Barley’s shoulder. All three of them are heartbroken.

speaker: LAUREL
text: I'm sorry you guys don't have your Dad here, but this shows just how much he wanted to see you both. So much that he’d try anything. That’s still a pretty special gift.

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah. Barley leaves.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Hey, want to come with me to pick up your cake?

speaker: IAN
text: That's okay. Thanks, Mom. As Laurel gets to the door, she looks back at Ian with a sad smile, then leaves. Ian sits on his bed, crestfallen.

speaker: IAN
text: Only once is all we get, grant me this rebirth... Suddenly, an orange light glows on Ian’s face. He turns to see the Phoenix Gem is glowing. He looks back at the spell.

speaker: IAN
text: 'Til tomorrow’s sun has set... The gem glows brighter. Objects in the room raise from the ground. The staff shakes and starts to fall. As Ian catches it, the staff lights up. He tightens his grip and reads louder.

speaker: IAN
text: One day to walk the earth! A beam of light shoots from the staff. Ian holds on with all his might as objects in the room swirl around him. Barley opens the door.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Hey man, what are you doing in here? Holy Tooth of Zadar! How did you--?

speaker: IAN
text: I don’t know, it just started! Blazey pokes her head in the door but quickly runs away. Ian strains to hold the staff. The light from the staff conjures a pair of shoes.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Whoa, feet! Above the shoes, bright purple socks appear. Then pant cuffs, pant legs, a belt, a shirt, one button at a time... The gem slowly lifts out of the staff and spins. An ominous red light swells from the gem, overpowering Ian. Dad’s body recedes to the waist. Ian braces with all he has.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Hang on, I can help! Barley reaches for the staff. Ian pulls away from Barley.

speaker: IAN
text: BARLEY, NO! The red swell explodes. The gem BURSTS into pieces.

speaker: IAN
text: Dad? The legs stand to reveal... they have no torso.

speaker: IAN
text: AH!

speaker: BARLEY
text: AH! He’s just legs! Ian and Barley cautiously approach the living pants as Dad’s feet begin to feel their way around the space.

speaker: BARLEY
text: There’s no top part. I definitely remember Dad having a top part!

speaker: IAN
text: Oh, what did I do? This is horrible! The pants start to make their way toward Ian and Barley. Ian calms himself, then approaches Dad’s pants. He reaches out, waving his hand through Dad’s missing torso.

speaker: IAN
text: Hello? Dad begins to feel around with his foot and walk blindly.

speaker: BARLEY
text: It’s really him. DAD! You are in your house! Dad bumps into a dresser and falls. The boys race over to help him up.

speaker: IAN
text: Whoa, whoa!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh boy!

speaker: IAN
text: Ah, he can’t hear us. Barley thinks, then walks over and taps on Dad’s foot: Shave and a Haircut.

speaker: IAN
text: What are you doing? Dad’s legs suddenly leap back with emotion - as much emotion as legs can have. He stomps the final two beats of the song. Dad touches Barley’s feet with his foot, patting gently.

speaker: BARLEY
text: That’s right Dad. It’s me, Barley. Dad feels around, finds Ian’s foot, and does the same.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Yeah, that’s Ian.

speaker: IAN
text: Hi... Dad. This lands on Ian, and he puts his hand delicately on Dad’s foot. It’s the closest they can get to a hug.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh... I messed this whole thing up and now he’s gonna be legs forever!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Well, not... forever. The spell only lasts one day. At sunset tomorrow, he’ll disappear and we’ll never be able to bring him back again. Barley points to the spell, illustrations of the sun setting and the man vanishing.

speaker: IAN
text: Okay, okay, okay. Twenty-Four hours...that doesn't give us much time, but-- Ian looks at his phone, broken in the chaos. He chucks it on his bed and sets his watch. Twenty-four hours counting down.

speaker: IAN
text: Well, we’ll just have to do the spell again.

speaker: BARLEY
text: You mean you have to. A person can only do magic if they have the gift. And my little brother has the magic gift! Barley pulls Ian into a playful headlock. Ian pushes away.

speaker: IAN
text: But I couldn’t even finish the spell.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Well, you're gonna have plenty of time to practice. Because we have to find another Phoenix Gem. Barley gets an idea. He flips through his Quests of Yore cards then holds one up to Ian. It shows an old pub: THE MANTICORE’S TAVERN.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ah-ha! We’ll start at the place where all quests begin. The Manticore’s Tavern!

speaker: BARLEY
text: It’s run by a fearless adventurer. She knows where to find any kind of gem, talisman, totem...

speaker: IAN
text: Barley, this is for a game.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Based on real life!

speaker: IAN
text: But, how do we know this tavern is still there?

speaker: BARLEY
text: It’s there. Look, my years of training have prepared me for this very moment. And I’m telling you... this is the only way to find a Phoenix Gem. Barley holds up a card featuring THE PHOENIX GEM.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Trust me. Ian looks longingly at Dad.

speaker: IAN
text: Whatever it takes, I am gonna meet my dad.

speaker: BARLEY
text: You hear that, Dad? We’re going on a quest!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Come on, Guinevere...

speaker: IAN
text: Uh... Maybe we should just take the bus.

speaker: BARLEY
text: She’s fine. The van starts. Barley grabs a cassette tape labeled “QUEST MIX” and pops it in the stereo. Sweeping fantasy music plays.

speaker: BARLEY
text: H’YAH! The van backfires and spits black smoke as it heads away from the cookie cutter suburban homes, toward the fantastical purple mountains in the distance.

speaker: IAN
text: Anyway, it’s like this award for math, it’s no big deal. But I’ll show you when we get back home. Barley looks in the rearview mirror.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Hey, what are you two Chatty Charlies up to back there?

speaker: IAN
text: You know, I felt weird talking to Dad without a top half, so... Ian picks something up and puts it on Dad, he steps aside to reveal it’s a top half made out of stuffed clothes.

speaker: IAN
text: ...Ta-da!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh! That’s great! Dad, you look just like I remember. Dad starts to wander toward the front seat.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Hey, don’t worry, we’ll have the rest of you here before you know it. And then, first thing I’m gonna do: introduce you to Guinevere. Rebuilt this old girl myself, from the lug nuts to the air conditioning. Barley flips on the air conditioning. It BLASTS like a leaf blower. Ian fights against the wind to turn it off.

speaker: IAN
text: Showing Dad your van? That’s your whole list?

speaker: BARLEY
text: What list?

speaker: IAN
text: Oh...

speaker: BARLEY
text: What’s that?

speaker: IAN
text: I’m just working on a list of things I wanted to do with Dad. You know, play catch, take a walk, driving lesson, share my whole life story with him. Barley nods, appreciative.

speaker: BARLEY
text: That’s cool. Oh, but before you cast Dad’s spell again, you’re gonna have to practice your magic. Barley tosses Ian the Quests of Yore book.

speaker: IAN
text: This book is for a game.

speaker: BARLEY
text: I told you, everything in Quests of Yore is historically accurate! Even the spells. So start practicing, young sorcerer! Barley hands Ian the staff. Ian opens the book.

speaker: IAN
text: Okay, Dad. Let’s try some magic.

speaker: IAN
text: Aloft Elevar. Nothing happens.

speaker: IAN
text: Aloft Elevar. Still nothing. Ian turns to Barley, picks up the Quests of Yore book gestures to a levitation spell in it.

speaker: IAN
text: I can’t get this levitation spell to work. Maybe I should try something else, like... Arcane Lightning?

speaker: BARLEY
text: Pfft, yeah, like a level one mage could bust out the hardest spell in the Enchanter’s Guide Book. Maybe we’ll stick with the easy ones.

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah, well, it’s not working. Am I saying it wrong?

speaker: BARLEY
text: You said it right, it’s just for any spell to work you have to speak from your heart’s fire.

speaker: IAN
text: My what?

speaker: BARLEY
text: Your heart's fire, you must speak with passion, don’t hold back. Ian seems doubtful about this advice.

speaker: IAN
text: Aloft Elevar! Nothing.

speaker: BARLEY
text: No, like - Aloft Elevar!

speaker: IAN
text: Aloft Elevar!

speaker: BARLEY
text: No, from your heart's fire!

speaker: IAN
text: ALOFT ELEVAR!

speaker: BARLEY
text: DON’T HOLD BACK!

speaker: IAN
text: ALOFT ELEVAR!

speaker: BARLEY
text: HEART'S FIRE! Ian tosses the staff in frustration.

speaker: IAN
text: STOP SAYING HEARTS FIRE! This just clearly isn’t working! Ian sits defeated next to Dad.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Hey, it was a good start. Ian stares at the Visitation spell, then looks to Dad, disappointed in himself.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh! Gather your courage, we’ve arrived. The Manticore’s Tavern. Ian looks out the window to see an old tavern, just like the one on the Quests of Yore card.

speaker: IAN
text: Wow, it is still here.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Yeah! I told you.

speaker: IAN
text: Come on, Dad! That’s good. They approach the ominous entrance, with a carving of the Manticore’s fearsome face above the door.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Alright, listen, first: let me do the talking. Secondly, it’s crucial we show the fearless Manticore the respect she deserves or she will thirdly, not give us a map to a Phoenix Gem.

speaker: IAN
text: Whoa, whoa, wait, the map?! I thought she had a Phoenix Gem?

speaker: BARLEY
text: You're so cute. Hear that, Dad? He’s a smart kid, he just doesn’t know how quests work.

speaker: IAN
text: Well, is there anything else you're forgetting to tell me? BARLEY - Nope. Barley pushes open the door.

speaker: WAITERS
text: Happy happy birthday, Come join us on our quest, To make your birthday party, The very, very best! HEY! Ian looks at Barley, losing faith in his brother.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Okay, okay, so the tavern changed a little over the years, but the Manticore is still the real deal. Ian and Barley walk through the restaurant, taking it all in. A TAVERN WAITRESS takes an order at a nearby table.

speaker: TAVERN PATRON
text: I’ll have the soup of the day.

speaker: TAVERN WAITRESS
text: Would m’lord like a Cup or Cauldron? An elf kid plays a claw drop game with a gauntlet hand picking up treasure. He loses. Barley marches up a Hostess.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Madame, I request an audience... ...with the Manticore!

speaker: HOSTESS
text: But of course, m’lord. The Hostess blows into a fake horn.

speaker: HOSTESS
text: Oh, Manticore! A plush COSTUMED MANTICORE leaps into frame. Adorable and silly, it lets out a CHUCKLE and hugs Ian. Barley pushes the Costumed Manticore away from Ian, irritated.

speaker: BARLEY
text: No, no, no, no no! The real Manticore! The fearless adventurer! The costumed Manticore rubs its eyes like it’s crying.

speaker: HOSTESS
text: Oh, you mean Corey? She’s over there. The hostess gestures toward the kitchen.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: QUICK, SOMEBODY HELP ME! These griffin nuggets were supposed to go out minutes ago! Waiters rushes in and she scrambles to offload the plates.

speaker: IAN
text: That's the Manticore? Barley leaps forward and takes a knee.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh, great and powerful Manticore!

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Whoah, sir! You’re right in the hot zone. A GOBLIN COOK passes by her.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: You’re late, Adolphus.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: I understand there’s traffic, you need to plan for that! The goblin heads onto the floor for his shift.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Well maybe your mother should get her own car!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Your fearlessness? The Manticore turns toward the boys, startled.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: AHH!

speaker: BARLEY
text: My brother and I seek a map... to a Phoenix Gem.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Oh, uh, well you've come to the right tavern. Table 12. She quickly races to a host podium and digs around.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: I have the parchment you desire right here! BEHOLD! She presents them with a children’s menu that says “FIND THE PHOENIX GEM!” above a variety of puzzles.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh... that’s a children’s menu.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Isn’t that fun? They’re all based on my old maps. Oh, uh, “The great Manticore sends you on your adventure with a hero’s blessing.” And here’s some crayons. She hurries off, but Barley follows reverently.

speaker: BARLEY
text: That’s very amusing your dominance, but might you have the real map? The Manticore points to a wall of swords, shields, scrolls.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Uh... yeah, it's over there. Ian points to a map on the wall with the title: PHOENIX GEM.

speaker: IAN
text: That's it!

speaker: BARLEY
text: This is perfect! Barley reaches for the scroll, but the Manticore grabs it.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Whoa, whoa, whoa! What are you doing? You can’t take this.

speaker: BARLEY
text: We have to. Barley removes Dad’s torso to reveal he’s just legs. The Manticore looks aghast, unsure what she’s seeing.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: What is that? The Manticore Mascot walks past and sees the legs. Stunned, the mascot bumps into a serving tray and FALLS to the ground.

speaker: IAN
text: It’s our dad, and we have a chance to meet him, but-- Barley steps in front of Ian, taking over.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Buuuut... we can’t do that without a Phoenix Gem. The Manticore considers this.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: No! My days of sending people on dangerous quests are over.

speaker: BARLEY
text: What? Why?

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Uh, cause they’re dangerous! The HOSTESS pops around the corner.

speaker: HOSTESS
text: Corey, the karaoke machine is broken again. The Manticore sees a group of female fantasy creatures in matching shirts surrounding a cyclops in a veil.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: I’m sorry, but you are not getting this map.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Don’t worry, ladies, your adventure will continue momentarily!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Okay, I can handle this. Ian pushes past Barley to approach the Manticore.

speaker: BARLEY
text: No, Ian! BRIDESMAID #1 You’re ruining Althea’s special day!

speaker: IAN
text: Miss... Mighty Manticore, ma’am--?

speaker: BARLEY
text: What are you doing?

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Kid, this is not a good time. The karaoke machine lets out a squall of feedback. BRIDESMAID #1 I’m giving this place a one-star review!

speaker: IAN
text: It’s just, I’ve never met my Dad and--

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Look, I’m sorry about that, but if you get hurt on one of my quests guess who gets sued and loses her tavern!? I can’t take that kind of risk! Now if you’ll just excuse me I have important things to do! Testing. You haunt my dreams, my inbetweens... Ian looks at Dad’s purple socks. He steps up.

speaker: IAN
text: Please, we need that map!

speaker: MANTICORE
text: No, I am not giving you the map! That's it! I’m done talking! Ian stands up straight and looks determined.

speaker: IAN
text: Well... well, I’m not!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Whoa, whoa, whoa! The Manticore looks up, surprised. Barley looks at Ian, equally surprised.

speaker: IAN
text: You say you can’t “risk” losing this place? Look at that Manticore! Ian points to a heroic painting of the warrior Manticore.

speaker: IAN
text: She looks like she lived to take risks!

speaker: MANTICORE
text: That Manticore didn't have investors to look out for. She didn't have payroll to cover! The Manticore stands up, gesturing wildly.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: She could just fly out the door whenever she wanted and slay a magma beast. BRIDESMAID #2 Are you gonna fix the machine or not?

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Yeah, in a minute! The startled bridesmaid backs off.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Okay, maybe this place isn’t AS adventurous as it used to be, so it isn’t filled with a motley horde willing to risk life and limb for the mere taste of excitement... A pathetic patron blows on his soup.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: ...but so what?! Whoever said you have to take risks in life to have an adventure?! Ian nervously points behind the Manticore.

speaker: IAN
text: Apparently, you did. The Manticore looks at her portrait, Above it is a plaque with that exact quote. The Manticore is speechless. A ROGUE WAITER walks up with a plate of mozzarella sticks.

speaker: ROGUE WAITER
text: Table 32 said their mozzarella sticks are cold. The Manticore breathes fire on the mozzarella sticks. Terrified, the waiter scuttles away.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: What have I done?

speaker: IAN
text: Well, it’s not too late. I mean, you could just give us the map--

speaker: MANTICORE
text: This place used to be dangerous... The Chuck E. Cheese-style Manticore mascot stands behind the real Manticore and begins to imitate her movements.

speaker: COSTUMED MANTICORE
text: Dangerous!

speaker: MANTICORE
text: ...and wild.

speaker: COSTUMED MANTICORE
text: Wild!

speaker: MANTICORE
text: I used to be dangerous and wild!

speaker: COSTUMED MANTICORE
text: Dangerous and wild! The Manticore stares daggers at the mascot moving behind her. She tackles it, rips off its foam head, and holds it up proudly with a mighty roar.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh no... The Manticore throws the smoldering, melting mascot head and it lands on a table between two customers.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: EVERYBODY OUT!! THIS TAVERN IS CLOSED FOR REMODELLING!!!! The mascot head ignites a banner that burns into the rafters.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Sorry, the karaoke machine is broken! Manticore throws the karaoke machine against the wall knocking away the dry wall and revealing the stone beneath. The Manticore picks up a cardboard standee of herself and throws it to the ground with a yell. She throws the Phoenix Gem map with it. Ian watches in horror as the map catches fire. He races to the map, but it’s too late. All that’s left is ashes.

speaker: IAN
text: No, no, no, no! No! ...No. A beam falls behind them.

speaker: BARLEY
text: We gotta go! They race through the restaurant, arm-in-arm with Dad, his torso detaches. His legs runs in the wrong direction, bump into a wall, and fall to the ground. Ian realizes they’ve lost Dad. He turns and sees Dad struggling to stand. The roof above suddenly buckles. The wooden beams CRASH down, headed right for Dad. Without a thought, Ian lifts the staff and calls out.

speaker: IAN
text: ALOFT ELEVAR! A blast of light from the staff freezes the wooden beam in mid-air, mere inches from Dad. Ian strains to hold the spell. Barley rushes in and grabs Dad, pulling him to safety. Ian releases the spell and the beam CRASHES to the ground. Barley, Ian, and Dad flee from the burning tavern.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ha-ha! That was unbelievable. You were just like... And the beam was just floatin' there! My brother is a wizard!

speaker: IAN
text: I can’t believe that worked.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh, you’re gonna nail Dad’s spell now.

speaker: IAN
text: Except we don’t have a map.

speaker: BARLEY
text: But we've got this! BEHOLD! Barley reveals a kids’ menu he grabbed. Ian sighs.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Look, on a quest, you have to use what you've got. And this is what we've got!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Best part is, little Kayla already solved the puzzle. Ian takes the menu. The puzzle has been solved in crayon.

speaker: IAN
text: Well, according to Kayla, we just have to look for “Raven’s Point.” Something dawns on him. Ian reaches down and takes out a map.

speaker: IAN
text: Raven’s Point... Raven’s Point... He slams a roadmap down on the dash and points to a mountain labeled: RAVEN’S POINT.

speaker: IAN
text: Raven’s Point!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Yes! The gem must be in the mountain. We can be there by tomorrow morning.

speaker: IAN
text: Tomorrow morning?

speaker: BARLEY
text: That still gives us plenty of time with Dad. Ian looks at Dad, disappointed.

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah. The van comes to a stop at a red light. Ian traces a route with his finger, right to Raven’s Point.

speaker: IAN
text: Well, it looks like the expressway should take us right there.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Eh, expressway is a little too obvious. On a quest, the clear path is never the right one--

speaker: IAN
text: What?

speaker: BARLEY
text: During one Quests of Yore campaign, Shrub Rosehammer and I took the easy route... led him straight into the belly of a gelatinous cube. Only reason I didn’t suffer the same fate? I followed my gut. Barley highlights barely-perceptible road on the map.

speaker: BARLEY
text: And it’s telling me we take an ancient trail called the Path of Peril.

speaker: IAN
text: But the expressway is faster.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Maybe not in the long run.

speaker: IAN
text: I know you want this to be like one of your adventure games, but all that matters is that we get to spend as much time as possible with Dad. Dad breaks free from his seatbelt in back and wanders toward the front of the van, leaving his top half behind. He taps his foot against Barley’s. Barley pats Dad’s foot and sighs.

speaker: IAN
text: So, we should just take the expressway. Right?

speaker: BARLEY
text: Yeah. You're right. But if you end up inside a gelatinous cube, you are on your own. Barley steers the van onto an onramp.

speaker: GPS VOICE
text: Manticore’s Tavern, ahead on your right. A phone call interrupts the GPS. It’s Colt. Laurel answers.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Hey.

speaker: COLT
text: I’m just checking in. Did you catch up to the boys yet?

speaker: LAUREL
text: No, not yet. But I’m a little worried because we had a weird family issue come up and, well, this just isn’t like Ian to run off. I mean, Barley, yes, but not Ian.

speaker: COLT
text: You know, it’s late, you shouldn’t have to be out looking for them.

speaker: LAUREL
text: I know, it’s silly, I’m sure they’re both probably on fire... Laurel sees the Manticore’s Tavern burning to the ground.

speaker: COLT
text: Fire?!

speaker: LAUREL
text: FIRE! The place is on fire! My boys, oh no, I gotta go!

speaker: COLT
text: Laurel!

speaker: COSTUMED MANTICORE
text: Get it off, get it off, get it off! The bachelorette party, disheveled and crying.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Excuse me? Hello! Please, I’m looking for two teenage elves?!

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Okay, okay, I told you already, there were two teenage elves...

speaker: LAUREL
text: Oh! Those are my sons! Where didthey go? The Manticore sits on the side of the curb, wrapped in a silver emergency blanket, holding a mug.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Oh, they went on a quest to find a Phoenix Gem. But don’t worry, don’t worry. I told them about the map, and about the gem, and about the curse. I forgot to tell them about the curse!

speaker: LAUREL
text: The what?

speaker: OFFICER AVEL
text: Hoo boy... A police officer, OFFICER AVEL, leans in to talk to Laurel.

speaker: OFFICER AVEL
text: Listen, this one’s gone a little

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Your boys are in grave danger! But I can help-- The Manticore starts to move toward Laurel. Officer Avel pulls the Manticore toward the police car.

speaker: OFFICER AVEL
text: Whoa, heyyy. You’re not going anywhere. We got questions for you.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: I know where they’re going! We can still save them! Laurel looks around, concerned. She’s sees a first aid kit in the back of an ambulance and gets an idea.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Last name “Manticore.” First name, “The.” Laurel walks up to Officer Avel and the Manticore.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Hold on! You’re right, she has gone a little– It’s no wonder with a wound like that. Laurel points dramatically to a slight scrape on the Manticore’s leg.

speaker: OFFICER AVEL
text: That’s just a scratch. Laurel pulls out the Quests of Yore card about the Manticore and waves it around like it’s an official document.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Oh, I'm sorry, are you an expert on minotaurs?

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Manticores.

speaker: LAUREL
text: --Manticores?

speaker: OFFICER AVEL
text: Well, no.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Well then you wouldn’t know that when their blood is exposed to air, it makes them go bonkers.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: I don’t think that’s true.

speaker: LAUREL
text: See! She’s already losing her grip on reality. So why don’t you let me save her life before it costs you yours? Officer Avel throws his hands in the air.

speaker: OFFICER AVEL
text: Okay!

speaker: LAUREL
text: Thank you! Could we have a little privacy here, please? Officer Avel steps away.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Just lie back. That’s good.

speaker: OFFICER AVEL
text: Just don’t take too long back there, okay? Hey, you hear me? Officer Avel turns and looks at the Manticore’s silhouette, then carefully walks back toward the police car.

speaker: OFFICER AVEL
text: I said, don’t take too long back there, because I-- As he gets closer, he sees the source of Manticore’s silhouette: The melted mascot head, propped up as a decoy.

speaker: OFFICER AVEL
text: Ahh!

speaker: LAUREL
text: Alright, how do we help my boys? The Manticore smiles.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Oooooh, I’m gonna like you!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Radio... headlights... brakes, tires, rims... I mean it’s hard to tell now, Dad, before I replaced her parts, Guinevere was actually kind of a piece of junk. Ian looks over at Dad and crosses things off his list.

speaker: IAN
text: Looks like we’re not gonna get to do everything today, Dad. But, that’s okay, I just wanna meet you. Dad rubs his foot against Ian’s. Ian smiles.

speaker: IAN
text: But don’t worry, we’ll have you fixed up and back home to see... Oh man, Mom! Barley, we’re not going to be able to get Dad back in time to see Mom.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh... Well, Dad... at least you won’t have to meet the new guy. Barley puts his finger up to his face to create a mustache.

speaker: BARLEY
text: So you, uh, workin’ hard or hardly workin’?

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah! Barley, Barley, Barley. Every time there’s trouble, I gotta deal with you.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Is that your Colt?!

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah?

speaker: BARLEY
text: You’re gonna wanna work on that. Suddenly the van begins to SPUTTER.

speaker: BARLEY
text: No, no, no, NO! Come on, old girl! Barley pulls the van off the expressway and lurches it to a stop on the side of the road.

speaker: IAN
text: I thought you said you fixed the van.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Relax, Guinevere is fine. Her stomach is just a little empty. Ian looks over at the gas gauge. The needle is on “F.”

speaker: IAN
text: But it says we have a full tank.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh, that doesn’t work.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Only a few drops left. Ian gets out. Barley climbs on top of Guinevere.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Maybe there’s a gas station... There’s nothing within sight. Ian looks at the staff.

speaker: IAN
text: Is there a magic way to get gas?

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh! I like your thinking young mage. Barley scrambles down from the van and shows Ian a page in the spell book: an illustration of a wizard growing an apple to five times its size.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Growth Spell! We grow the can and then the gas inside will grow with it.

speaker: IAN
text: Uhh... that’s kind of a weird idea...

speaker: BARLEY
text: I know! I like it too! Ian mutters to himself as he holds the spell and the staff.

speaker: IAN
text: Okay, loosen up, heart’s fire. Here we go.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Whoa, it’s not that simple. This one learns a little magic and thinks he’s Shamblefoot the Wondrous, am I right, Dad? A growth spell is a bit more advanced. Not only do you have to speak from your heart's fire, but now you also have to follow a magic decree.

speaker: IAN
text: A magic what? Barley shows Ian the book.

speaker: BARLEY
text: It's a special rule that keeps the spell working right. This one states: “To magnify an object, you have to magnify your attention upon it.” While you cast the spell, you can’t let anything distract you. Barley puts the gas can on the ground. Ian points the staff.

speaker: IAN
text: Okay... Ow!

speaker: BARLEY
text: What?

speaker: IAN
text: Splinter! Can we sand this thing down?

speaker: BARLEY
text: No! It’s an ancient staff with magic in every glorious fiber, you can’t “sand it down.”

speaker: IAN
text: Alright, alright. Here we go. Focus... Ian holds out the staff and tries to focus.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Uh...

speaker: IAN
text: Something wrong?

speaker: BARLEY
text: Sorry, it’s just, your stance is uh... Here. Barley poses Ian awkwardly, all over his personal space.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Chin up, elbows out, feet apart, back slightly arched... Okay, how’s that feel?

speaker: IAN
text: Great.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh! One more thing.

speaker: IAN
text: Barley!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Okay! Okay. Barley tries to adjust Ian’s elbow one more time. Ian shoots him a look. Barley throws up his hands and steps back. Ian focuses hard and recites the spell.

speaker: IAN
text: Magnora Gantuan! The staff throws off violent, flashing sparks. Ian winces, but does his best to stay focused. The can begins to grow.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Don’t let the magic spook you.

speaker: IAN
text: Okay.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Elbows!

speaker: IAN
text: What?!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Elbows up! Ian lifts his elbows as high as possible.

speaker: BARLEY
text: No, no, no, too high. That’s too high.

speaker: IAN
text: I’m trying to focus here!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh, yeah, yeah, yeah! Focus on the can!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Focuuuuuus... Focuuuuuuuuuussssss...

speaker: IAN
text: Barley! Ah, forget it! Ian lowers the staff, frustrated.

speaker: BARLEY
text: It worked! The can is huge! Ian looks down to see that Barley is now TINY.

speaker: BARLEY
text: And the van is huge! And you’re-- Barley looks at his tiny body.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh, no...

speaker: IAN
text: What happened?

speaker: BARLEY
text: Looks like you shrunk me.

speaker: IAN
text: Wha-- how?!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Well, if you mess up a spell, there are consequences.

speaker: IAN
text: I only messed up because you wouldn’t stop bothering me!

speaker: BARLEY
text: I was trying to help you!

speaker: IAN
text: Well, don’t try to help me!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh okay, fine, I won’t! As the boys argue, Dad taps Ian’s foot then searches for Barley. Dad taps Barley’s head, nearly stepping on him.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Whoa, ow! Dad, it’s me! Barley does “Shave and a Haircut” on Dad’s foot. Dad freaks out, recognizing him and reacting to his shrunken size.

speaker: IAN
text: Whoah, Dad, it’s okay, don’t worry, I’m going fix this! Ian grabs Dad’s leash and picks up the gas can.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Well, where are you going?

speaker: IAN
text: To find a gas station.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Well, I’m going too.

speaker: IAN
text: Fine. Ian tries to pick up Barley, but Barley refuses.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Hey, I don’t need your help!

speaker: IAN
text: Fine with me. Ian struggles to carry everything as Barley runs after Ian.

speaker: BARLEY
text: I just need a little break. My baby legs can’t go that fast. Ian walks back and awkwardly picks up Barley by the shirt.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Look, a gas station! Ian turns to see a glowing gas station in the distance.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh, good.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh wait, I forgot. You don’t need my help. Ian drops Barley into his pocket and charges ahead.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Hey! I don’t need you to carry me! I’m a grown man!

speaker: IAN
text: Dad, come on, it’s okay, Barley is with me. Barley looks at his reflection in the gas pump.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Yeah, I’m fine, Dad. The side effects are supposed to wear off eventually. Do I look any bigger yet? A group of loudly revving, seemingly unmanned motorcycles park in front of the gas station. Suddenly, a sprite in a leather jacket, DEWDROP, pops out from behind the bike. Other sprites appear. They were all collectively driving the bikes. They hop off and swagger into the gas station. An elf walks out of the gas station drinking a slushie. He unknowingly hits Dewdrop with the door on his way out.

speaker: DEWDROP
text: Hey! Did you just bump into me?

speaker: GAS STATION VICTIM
text: Oh, I’m terribly sorry, I didn’t see you there--

speaker: DEWDROP
text: Do it again, and you’ll see me in your nightmares! He drops his slushie and runs away.

speaker: COBWEB
text: Gimme a bag of Extra Sours. The clerk hands over a bag.

speaker: COBWEB
text: Keep the change. The sprites pass Ian, carrying a box of candy-filled straws.

speaker: COBWEB
text: Outta the way, beanstalk. Ian tries to keep a low profile as he talks to the CLERK.

speaker: IAN
text: Uh, ten on pump two, please. Barley leans out of Ian’s pocket, reaching for a bag of snacks, but knocks over the entire rack.

speaker: IAN
text: What are you doing?!

speaker: BARLEY
text: I’m getting us food. Ian pushes a few bags of cheese puffs forward.

speaker: IAN
text: Alright, I got it. And a couple of these, thank you.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Pssst! I have to go to the bathroom!

speaker: IAN
text: Can it wait?

speaker: BARLEY
text: It’s your pocket. Ian turns back to the clerk.

speaker: IAN
text: Can we have the bathroom key, please? The clerk hands them a bathroom key attached to a license plate. Ian takes Barley out of his pocket and gives him the key. Tiny Barley has to carry the key over his head.

speaker: IAN
text: Okay, be quick. I want to get out of here.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Okay, I’m going. Barley runs to the bathroom. Dad searches around with his foot for little Barley and just misses him.

speaker: GAS STATION CLERK
text: That’ll be twelve ninety-nine. Ian hands money to the cashier. Dad continues to search for little Barley, and taps the head of one of the sprites.

speaker: MUSTARDSEED
text: Hey! Watch it! Dewdrop leaps over to Dad and puffs out her chest.

speaker: DEWDROP
text: You got a problem, Shades?! Dad stares at her.

speaker: DEWDROP
text: Answer me when I’m talking to you! Ian turns to see that Dewdrop staring down Dad from high on a shelf. Ian grabs Dad and pulls him away.

speaker: IAN
text: Sorry, I don’t really know where his head is at right now. Ian pulls Dad to the door and EXITS.

speaker: IAN
text: How could this night get any worse?

speaker: MANTICORE
text: You know, I would fly us to help your boys, but... the old wings aren’t what they used to be.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Oh, that’s fine. So, about this curse-- The Manticore wiggles her wings a little.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: It’s my own fault. I should be doing my wing exercises every morning, but you know how that goes...

speaker: LAUREL
text: Please, the curse. What does it do?

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Right! Sorry. It’s a Guardian Curse. The Manticore rips off her sleeve to reveal an arm full of tattoos. She points to an image of a red cloud.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: If your boys take the gem, the curse will rise up... She points to the red mist holding rocks together to make a beast attacking someone.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: ...and assume the form of a mighty beast... and battle your sons to The... Well... how do your boys do in a crisis?

speaker: LAUREL
text: Not great! One of them is afraid of everything and the other isn’t afraid of anything.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Yeah, that skinny kid of yours is pretty fearless.

speaker: LAUREL
text: No, no. You mean the big one. Barley.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: No, the little guy. Woo, he really let me have it.

speaker: LAUREL
text: What? No, look... You said you could help them. Right? The Manticore points to a glowing circle in the “heart” of one of the beasts.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Every curse has a core, the center of its power. And only one weapon forged of the rarest metals can destroy it: my enchanted sword... The Manticore rips off her other sleeve to reveal a tattoo of a glowing yellow sword that reads: THE CURSE CRUSHER.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: ...the Curse Crusher!

speaker: LAUREL
text: Okay, but you don’t seem to have that on you. The Manticore looks at Laurel, embarrassed.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: I sold it. Got in a little tax trouble a few years back. But don’t worry, I know just where to find it. Laurel nods, determined.

speaker: LAUREL
text: I am on my way boys, just try to stay out of trouble.

speaker: IAN
text: Ugh, what is taking you so long? Then he hears something off in the distance.

speaker: DEWDROP
text: Who you calling whimsical? Ian looks over to see Barley in talking to two sprites.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh no.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Whoa, whoa, whoa!

speaker: DEWDROP
text: You got a lotta nerve. Ian makes his way toward Barley while struggling with Dad.

speaker: BARLEY
text: I’m just saying sprites used to fly around spreading delight. That’s a good thing.

speaker: DEWDROP
text: Sprites can’t fly!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Well, your wings don’t work 'cause you stopped using them.

speaker: DEWDROP
text: You calling me lazy? She throws down her candy straw on the ground and stomps it.

speaker: BARLEY
text: No, no, no. Not you. Your ancestors.

speaker: DEWDROP
text: What did you say about my ancestors?!

speaker: BARLEY
text: I didn't mean lazy, I-- But Ian swoops in and GRABS Barley while also holding the full gas can and a wandering Dad.

speaker: IAN
text: I’m sorry! Very sorry! He’s sorry, too. You don’t need to fly, who needs to fly? I mean you’ve got those great bikes!

speaker: BARLEY
text: What are you doing? I was just discussing history!

speaker: IAN
text: Barley, I’m trying to take care of you and Dad, and you are not making it any easier! Dad, come on! Dad has wandered to the other side of the line of motorcycles and Ian unwittingly pulls him into the bikes. They CRASH like dominoes, blocking the gas station door.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh no. The Sprites inside the gas station SLAM into the door and windows of the station. They are SEETHING caged animals.

speaker: DEWDROP
text: HEY! YOU’RE DEAD! Ian RACES Dad and Barley out of there as fast as he can.

speaker: IAN
text: We’re dead! We’re dead! We’re dead! We’re dead! We’re dead! Ian frantically fills the van with gasoline.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Relax, they won’t be able to lift those bikes-- One motorcycle ROARS to life in the distance.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh, they are strong...

speaker: IAN
text: We’re gonna die, we’re gonna die, we’re gonna die! Barley jumps up and down on the door handle.

speaker: BARLEY
text: It’s locked!

speaker: IAN
text: What?! Where are the keys? They see the keys still in the ignition.

speaker: BARLEY
text: I got this! Barley slides his tiny body through the cracked window. He struggles to try to lift the lock.

speaker: IAN
text: C’mon, Barley! The lock pops up, sending Barley flying backwards.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Whoa! Ian climbs in the passenger side and slams the door.

speaker: IAN
text: Okay, Go! Go! Go! Ian looks at Barley, tiny and useless on the drivers seat.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh, no. No, no, no, no. No way...

speaker: BARLEY
text: You’re gonna have to! Ian slides over into the drivers seat. He carefully buckles his seatbelt, but then sees the motorcycles coming. Ian turns the key. The engine whines, but nothing happens.

speaker: IAN
text: Come on, why won’t it start?!

speaker: BARLEY
text: There’s a sweet spot... not in the middle, but not quite at the end! Ian wiggles the ignition, leaning into it.

speaker: IAN
text: Comeoncomeoncomeoncomeon! Come. On. Guinevere! The van sputters to life.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Put it in “O” for “onward!” Ian pulls the screwdriver gear shift down to the handwritten letter “O”. The van lurches forward just as a sprite SMASHES out Ian’s window with a flail.

speaker: IAN
text: AHH!

speaker: BARLEY
text: DRIVE!!! Ian’s unintentionally cuts off the sprites. Their bikes get tangled up as the van speeds away.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Okay, you’re gonna have to merge! Cars ROAR past the van as Ian cautiously picks up speed. A huge truck barrels up beside him. Ian tentatively steers the van closer to the freeway. The truck lays on its horn.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Speed up!

speaker: IAN
text: I can’t do this!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Yes you can!

speaker: IAN
text: I’m not ready!

speaker: BARLEY
text: You’ll never be ready! MERGE!!

speaker: IAN
text: AHHH!!! Ian floors it and cuts in front of the giant truck.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ha-ha! Nice job! In the rearview mirror, Ian sees the motorcycles gaining on them. They pull up on both sides of the van, surrounding him. A biker pulls out a flail and hits the van. Barley leaps up to the window, yelling out at the sprites.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Hey, don’t hit Gwinny!

speaker: IAN
text: Barley! Ian quickly comes up behind a slow moving car.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh, no!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Get around ’em!

speaker: IAN
text: They're not letting me in!

speaker: BARLEY
text: SIGNAL!

speaker: IAN
text: You don’t have a signal-er!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Stick your arm straight out the window to signal left! Ian holds his arm straight out the window. A sprite immediately wraps a chain around it.

speaker: IAN
text: AH!! Ian turns the wheel one-handed and merges into the next lane. Barley climbs out on Ian’s arm to untangle the chain.

speaker: IAN
text: Get back here!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Just keep driving! The sprites cheer as they gain on the van.

speaker: IAN
text: Barley! Barley successfully untangles the chain. It recoils and almost knocks a number of sprites off their motorcycle. Ian looks ahead and sees the freeway splitting EAST and NORTH. Barley crawls back onto Ian’s shoulder.

speaker: BARLEY
text: The mountains are north. You need to get all the way over! Dad stumbles to the front of the car and face plants into the steering wheel, blaring Guinevere’s horn.

speaker: DEWDROP
text: What the-- Oh it is on, Shades! Get 'em! A motorcycle races up to Ian’s window. A group of sprites LEAP from their bike and into the van, attacking Ian.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ian! Stay focused!

speaker: IAN
text: I can’t!

speaker: BARLEY
text: You HAVE to focus or we're all dead! Ian sees the NORTH sign out the window rapidly approaching.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Just stay cool! Ian gets an idea. He frantically reaches and turns on the AC. Strong air blasts from the vents, blowing the sprites out the windows. The sprite that was fighting Barley is blown into the windshield of the car behind Guinevere. Barley clings to the seatbelt, flapping in the wind.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Way to go, Guinevere! He loses his grip and is suddenly blown out the window. But Ian manages to grab him at the last minute. The sprites rev their bike for another attack.

speaker: DEWDROP
text: Hit it! Ian sees his exit ahead, across several lanes.

speaker: BARLEY
text: We’re not gonna make it! With determination, Ian crosses several lanes of traffic, just barely making it onto the NORTH fork of the freeway. The sprites don’t have time to correct their course. They crash their bike and hurtle toward the ground. But their wings flap until suddenly... they’re flying.

speaker: DEWDROP
text: Huh.

speaker: IAN
text: Dad, are you okay?!

speaker: BARLEY
text: He’s fine, thanks to the skillful driving of Sir Ian Lightfoot! High Five! Barley raises his hand to high five, but it bulges to full size and smacks Ian in the face.

speaker: IAN
text: Ow! What is happening?

speaker: BARLEY
text: I think the spell is wearing off! Barley’s torso expands to full size on Ian’s shoulder. Ian can’t even see out the window to know where he’s going.

speaker: IAN
text: Get off my face! Barley’s right leg grows, slamming onto the gas pedal.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh, Chantar’s Talon! Cops!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Pull over!

speaker: IAN
text: I don’t have a license. Barley pulls a small piece of folded leather from his pocket.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ah! My wallet’s still tiny!

speaker: OFFICER SPECTER
text: Step out of the vehicle.

speaker: IAN
text: What are we gonna do?

speaker: BARLEY
text: I don’t know! In the background, Dad’s dizzy legs stumble around, then lean against the open back doors of the van and begin to step out.

speaker: IAN
text: How are we gonna explain– Oh no. DAD!

speaker: OFFICER GORE
text: You have a long night there, buddy?

speaker: OFFICER SPECTER
text: Sir, I'm gonna ask you to walk this straight line. Meanwhile, Ian and Barley confer in front of the van.

speaker: IAN
text: They’re gonna take Dad! Barley thinks for a moment.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh! I got it! The disguise spell! You can disguise yourself to be anyone you want. Barley draws the spell in the dirt.

speaker: IAN
text: What if I mess up again?

speaker: BARLEY
text: According to the spell, “Disguising yourself is a lie, so you must tell the truth to get by.” As long as you don’t tell a lie, the spell will be fine.

speaker: IAN
text: Okay... who are we gonna be? The cops watch a wandering, meandering Dad.

speaker: OFFICER GORE
text: Okay, we’re taking you down to the station. There’s a flash followed by the sound of hooves. The cops turn to see Officer Colt Bronco walk out from behind the van.

speaker: COLT
text: What seems to be the problem here fellow... police folk? Colt’s back legs walk out of sync, nervous and awkward.

speaker: OFFICER GORE
text: Officer Bronco?

speaker: OFFICER SPECTER
text: Were you in that van? To the side of Colt we see a new point of view: The boys are masked by a ghost-like shell of Colt, Ian in the front, Barley in the back. We hear Colt’s voice turn into Ian’s.

speaker: COLT/IAN
text: Affirmative! And we will, I mean, I will take full responsibility for that fella right there, so you can just release him over to me.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Hey, I wanted to be the front.

speaker: IAN
text: No way, I’ll do the talking.

speaker: OFFICER GORE
text: Bronco, I thought you were working on the other side of town.

speaker: COLT
text: I, uh, changed my mind. Suddenly Colt’s right ear vanishes. Ian quickly turns, blocking the missing ear from view.

speaker: OFFICER SPECTER
text: Something wrong?

speaker: COLT
text: Just a little... neck... cramp! And with that, Colt’s left hand goes away. Ian quickly and awkwardly bends his arm behind his body.

speaker: BARLEY
text: You have to stop lying. Answer every question with a question!

speaker: OFFICER SPECTER
text: What exactly are you doing out here?

speaker: COLT
text: Uh... What am I doing out here? What are any of us doing out here?

speaker: OFFICER GORE
text: Whoa... I never thought about it like that.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Nice!

speaker: OFFICER SPECTER
text: With all due respect, you didn’t answer my question.

speaker: COLT
text: Well, we were just exercising some driver’s education drills for... Ian.

speaker: OFFICER SPECTER
text: Who is Ian?

speaker: OFFICER GORE
text: Oh, is that Laurel’s kid?

speaker: COLT
text: Ian is Laurel’s kid.

speaker: OFFICER SPECTER
text: Your stepson was swerving all over the road.

speaker: COLT/IAN
text: Yeah, well... that guy’s not all there today. They all look to see Dad slump on the hood of the police car.

speaker: OFFICER SPECTER
text: Yeah, he does seem a little off. You seem a little off yourself. Behind the illusion, Ian is nervous and sweating.

speaker: IAN/COLT
text: Uh, actually, if I’m being completely honest... I’m not super great in this kinda situation... and I’m starting to freak out a little bit. I’m all sweaty and weird and I don’t know what to say and I just feel like I can’t do anything right and I’m a total weirdo-- Specter and Gore share a look. Specter takes Colt aside.

speaker: OFFICER SPECTER
text: Whoa, whoa, hold on, okay hold on, hold on. I think I know what’s going on here.

speaker: IAN
text: Uh... You do?

speaker: OFFICER SPECTER
text: Yeah, it’s not easy being a new parent. My girlfriend’s daughter got me pulling my hair out, okay?

speaker: IAN
text: Oh... uh, yeah.

speaker: OFFICER SPECTER
text: Alright, we can let him go.

speaker: COLT
text: Oh, okay... I'm just gonna take him to the van.

speaker: OFFICER SPECTER
text: Hey, it gets better, alright? Good luck, Bronco.

speaker: COLT
text: You too, Officer. Keep workin’ hard... or hardly workin’... The boys, still disguised as Colt, put Dad back in the van.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Now that was a good Colt! Ian grins at Barley.

speaker: OFFICER GORE
text: I don’t envy you, Bronco. That Lightfoot kid is a handful.

speaker: COLT
text: I'm gonna have to disagree with you there, I think Ian's a pretty stand up citizen.

speaker: OFFICER GORE
text: Not him, the older one.

speaker: IAN
text: What?

speaker: OFFICER GORE
text: I mean, the guy’s a screw up. You can’t say you don’t agree?

speaker: IAN
text: Um... I don’t. Colt’s right leg instantly vanishes. Right in front of Barley’s face.

speaker: BARLEY
text: What?

speaker: IAN/COLT
text: I mean, um, uh, okay, well, um, I have to get going, gotta get Ian home. Colt’s other arm vanishes. Ian quickly shuffles back to the van. The cops watch, baffled by Colt’s behavior.

speaker: COLT
text: Ah, I mean, I’m late for work. Colt’s back legs vanish.

speaker: COLT/IAN
text: Sorry, I mean I’m feeling a little sick. I mean, tired. Sick and tired. I gotta go! See you Monday! With each lie, Colt loses an arm, leg, ear. Ian and Barley just barely slink into the van before Colt’s head disappears.

speaker: OFFICER GORE
text: Oh man, Bronco is losing it. See, that’s why I never got married. Yup, Old Gore can’t be tied down. Specter watches the van drive away. Then she CLICKS on her flashlight and follows Colt’s hoof prints in the dirt, only to find them eventually lead to regular shoe prints.

speaker: OFFICER SPECTER
text: This is Specter. Can you put me through to Officer Colt Bronco?

speaker: IAN
text: Barley... I don’t know what happened back there, but I don’t think you’re a screwup. Barley says nothing.

speaker: IAN
text: Maybe the magic just got it wrong... you know?

speaker: BARLEY
text: Yeah... Barley BLASTS super loud fantasy metal. Ian winces.

speaker: IAN
text: I DON’T KNOW WHAT HAPPENED-- Barley turns up the music louder.

speaker: IAN
text: BARLEY! BARLEY! Suddenly Barley whips off the road at a rest area. He gets out of the van through the side door.

speaker: IAN
text: Where are you going? Ian follows after him, calling out.

speaker: IAN
text: Barley, come on, this is all just-- Barley stops and turns to Ian.

speaker: BARLEY
text: I’m not a screwup!

speaker: IAN
text: I didn’t say you were.

speaker: BARLEY
text: The magic said it for you!

speaker: IAN
text: Well, the magic got it wrong!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Magic doesn’t get it wrong! The cop asked a question, you answered, and magic revealed the truth. Right?! RIGHT?!

speaker: IAN
text: I don’t know how any of this stuff works! All I know is that everything we’ve done tonight has gone wrong!

speaker: BARLEY
text: It’s gone wrong because you won’t listen to me! Ian stops, stunned.

speaker: IAN
text: Are you kidding? Because everything we’ve done has been your idea--

speaker: BARLEY
text: But you didn’t do it my way! You didn’t let me handle the Manticore. You freaked out when I talked to the sprites. Because you don’t think I have good ideas.

speaker: IAN
text: What?! Of course I do!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Great, then I think we should take the Path of Peril!

speaker: IAN
text: And I also think that would be good... normally--

speaker: BARLEY
text: See!

speaker: IAN
text: But I told you, this isn’t a game! All that matters today is Dad, and right now he’s sitting in that van, and he’s confused-- Barley looks over Ian’s shoulder back toward the van.

speaker: IAN
text: What?! Ian turns to see Dad standing in the back of the van, bending his knees to the music.

speaker: IAN
text: What is he doing?

speaker: BARLEY
text: I think he can feel the vibrations of the music and he's... dancing. The boys watch as Dad slowly adds more dance moves. Then Dad really cuts loose and it's... not good.

speaker: IAN
text: Wow. He is terrible. Dad flails so wildly that his top half falls off.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Yeah. He's really, really bad. They watch in embarrassment. Dad shimmies over to the boys.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh no, here he comes. Dad hooks his leg around Barley’s, cajoling him to dance.

speaker: BARLEY
text: No no no, thanks Dad. Dad, thank you! I’m good! Stop! Okay, okay! Ian watches Barley and Dad dance. Then Dad walks over to Ian.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh! No, no, no. No, no, no! I’m not really a big dancer-- Ian tries to get away, but Dad won’t have it. Ian is forced to dance. The boys both dance with Dad.

speaker: IAN
text: Just imagine what the top half of this dance looks like.

speaker: BARLEY
text: I bet it goes something like this! Barley dances wildly behind Dad, mimicking Dad’s upper half for a moment. The boys laugh. The music stops. Dad sits.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh, you danced your shoelaces loose there, Pop. Barley kneels to tie Dad’s shoe. Then speaks to Ian without looking at him.

speaker: BARLEY
text: You know, I want to see him, too.

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah... I know. Barley stands.

speaker: BARLEY
text: It’s not fair for you to call me a screwup if you don’t give me a chance to get something right. Just do one thing my way. This hits Ian.

speaker: IAN
text: You really think this “Path of Peril” is the best way to go to the mountain? Barley nods. Ian looks uncertain, then...

speaker: IAN
text: Okay. Barley smiles.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Uh, so where is your magic sword?

speaker: MANTICORE
text: It lies beyond those gates. The MANTICORE looks toward a small building with a flashing sign that reads: PAWN SHOP.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: If we don't leave here with the sword, your boys are doomed.

speaker: GRECKLIN
text: There you are, one garlic crusher.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: No, Curse Crusher. It's a large magical sword. Grecklin looks through junk behind the counter.

speaker: GRECKLIN
text: Sword, sword, sword... She holds up a giant, glorious sword.

speaker: GRECKLIN
text: I mean, I got this thing.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: That’s it!

speaker: LAUREL
text: How much?

speaker: GRECKLIN
text: Let’s call it, uh... ten.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Great! Laurel counts the money. The Manticore speaks to her sword.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Forged of the rarest metals, the only sword of its kind in all the land. Hello, old friend. We shall never part again. Grecklin slams her hand on the sword.

speaker: GRECKLIN
text: Oops. Turns out this sword is the only sword of its kind in all the land. So, let’s call it ten... thousand.

speaker: LAUREL
text: You can’t do that!

speaker: GRECKLIN
text: Well, I just did.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Well you had better-- Laurel’s phone rings. She steps away to answer it.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Yeah.

speaker: COLT
