text: Hey, I talked to some other officers and they said the boys were last seen going north.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Are they okay?

speaker: COLT
text: They’re fine, but the officers said, well... honey, this night keeps getting stranger and stranger. Laurel looks over to see the Manticore and Grecklin arguing.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Do you know who I am?

speaker: GRECKLIN
text: Some kind of winged bear-snake lady?

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Winged lion-scorpion lady! Back on Laurel.

speaker: LAUREL
text: It sure does. Laurel walks over to Grecklin and the Manticore, putting down her phone for a moment.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Listen, I need that sword. My sons have a once-in-a-lifetime chance to see their father. Now, my oldest son-- The Manticore’s tail suddenly STRIKES Grecklin in the neck. Grecklin’s eyes POP open and she falls to the ground.

speaker: LAUREL
text: HOLY- SON OF- YOU KILLED HER!

speaker: MANTICORE
text: It's okay! She’s only temporarily paralyzed. On the other side of the counter, Grecklin lies motionless.

speaker: GRECKLIN
text: HEY! You can’t do this!

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Well, I just did. Laurel considers this for a moment.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Grab the sword!

speaker: GRECKLIN
text: Don’t you touch that! The Manticore grabs the Curse Crusher from the counter. Laurel delicately puts some cash on the counter.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Here you go. And a little something extra for your trouble. I love your store! Oh, that’s so pretty. Laurel flips the sign to “CLOSED” and exits.

speaker: GRECKLIN
text: Hey! HEY!

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Ohhh yeah!

speaker: COLT
text: Laurel?! What’s happening?! Back on Colt, who looks panicked.

speaker: COLT
text: Hello? Are you alright?! Laurel!

speaker: LAUREL
text: Oh Colt, I can't talk! The boys need me! And she hangs up on Colt.

speaker: COLT
text: Wait! Dang those kids. Just then, Colt sees a bumper at the head of a rough path. He shines his flashlight onto the plate reading “GWNIVER”. Colt’s hoof HITS the gas.

speaker: BARLEY
text: We’re heading on our quest, our father we must retrieve, the Lightfoot Brothers can’t be stopped... something, something that rhymes with retrieved! A bump jostles Ian awake in back. Dad also wakes up in a panic. He taps Ian’s foot.

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah, we’re still here. Dad goes to the front and taps his foot against Barley.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Well, good morning to thee, dear Lightfoot men! Welcome to the Path of Peril! Ian looks out the window at a desolate field.

speaker: IAN
text: It’s not much of a path.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Well, you know, they never really developed around here. So heads up, we could run into anything. A centicore, a wolf dragon, gelatinous cube...

speaker: IAN
text: Okay, what is a gelatinous cube? Barley turns to Ian, taking his eyes off the road.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh, it’s a giant green cube that instantly disintegrates all that it touches!

speaker: IAN
text: We’re not going to run into a– WHOA, STOP!! Barley SLAMS on the brakes. The van comes to a screeching halt at the edge of a chasm.

speaker: IAN
text: What is this?

speaker: BARLEY
text: Bottomless pit. Whatever falls in there, falls forever. Ian peers over. He doesn’t notice Dad wander right to the edge. Ian pulls him back at the last minute.

speaker: IAN
text: Whoa, Dad, come here. Ian walks Dad back the van and places him in the front seat.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ian, check it out. This is an ancient drawbridge! We lower that bad boy and we are on our way to Raven’s Point. Look around for a lever. Ian points.

speaker: IAN
text: Found it! But, it’s on the other side. Ian gets an idea. He lifts the staff.

speaker: IAN
text: Okay, I got this. Aloft Elevar. Magic shoots from the staff, but peters out halfway across.

speaker: BARLEY
text: You can’t cast a levitation spell on something that far away! It only has like a 15-meter enchanting radius. Dad, can you believe this guy? What we need is a Trust Bridge. It's a spell that creates a magical bridge you can walk on. Just say "Bridgrigar Invisia."

speaker: IAN
text: Okay... Ian walks to the lip of the chasm and holds out his staff.

speaker: IAN
text: Bridgrigar Invisia. The staff lights up, but no bridge. Ian looks at Barley.

speaker: IAN
text: It didn’t work.

speaker: BARLEY
text: No, the spell's still going. You won’t know if your bridge worked until you step on it.

speaker: IAN
text: Step on what?

speaker: BARLEY
text: If you believe the bridge is there, then it’s there.

speaker: IAN
text: But it’s not.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Well, not with that attitude. Ian gestures to the empty air over the chasm.

speaker: IAN
text: I’m not going to step out onto nothing! Barley considers this, then gets an idea.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Now we've got a rope. But you’re not even going to need it because--

speaker: IAN
text: I WANT THE ROPE!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Okay, I’m just saying, you’re not gonna need the rope because I know you can make that bridge. Ian takes a deep breath and gathers himself. He takes a big step and... FALLS over the edge.

speaker: IAN
text: Agh! Barley pulls the rope and stops Ian’s fall.

speaker: IAN
text: I’m dying, I’m dying! I’m dead! I’m dead! I’m dead! Oh, my life is over!

speaker: BARLEY
text: I’ve got you! Barley pulls Ian up, scraping him along the rocks and roots of the chasm. When he gets to the top, Barley dusts him off.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Okay, you fell. But was that so bad?

speaker: IAN
text: YES!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Are you still alive?

speaker: IAN
text: Yes.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Okay, so now you know the worst that can happen. So there’s nothing to be scared of, right? Ian thinks about this and looks across the chasm. He takes a breath and repositions himself at the edge.

speaker: IAN
text: Bridgrigar Invisia! The staff glows. Ian looks at the chasm, then back at Barley.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Hey. You can do this. He STEPS off the edge. But this time his foot stops mid-air, planted on a glowing platform of light.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ha-ha!

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah!

speaker: BARLEY
text: THERE YOU GO! Ian stands there for a moment, one-legged, then plants his other foot. But this foot doesn’t land on anything. Barley gives a tug on the rope and pulls him back.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Believe with every step! Ian closes his eyes, concentrates, then LANDS it on another platform of light.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh yeah! Ian takes another step. It lands, solid. He takes another few steps and calls back to Barley.

speaker: IAN
text: You’ve got me, right?

speaker: BARLEY
text: I still got you! WOOHOO! Ian Lightfoot is FEARLESS! Ian takes one step after another to the center of the chasm. But the knot in the rope loosens. Barley’s eyes go wide.

speaker: IAN
text: This is amazing! The rope loosens more and more, until it falls free, dropping into the chasm.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Yeah, but just keep going! Don’t look back! Just straight ahead!

speaker: IAN
text: You’ve still got the rope, right? Barley looks at the useless rope in his hands.

speaker: BARLEY
text: YEAH! I GOT IT!

speaker: IAN
text: I AM NOT AFRAID! Oh man, I could stay out here all day! Ian playfully steps from side to side.

speaker: BARLEY
text: OKAY, BUT KEEP MOVING! WE’VE GOTTA SEE DAD, REMEMBER?! Ian approaches the other side of the chasm.

speaker: IAN
text: HEY DAD, THIS LAST STEP IS FOR YOU! Ian turns to look at Barley and Dad. When he does, he sees the rope isn’t attached to him anymore. As soon as he sees this, he FALLS. But just catches himself on the lip of the chasm. He pulls himself up, terrified, and leans against the bridge lever.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ha-ha! He did it, Dad! Barley drives across the bridge and gets out to hug Ian.

speaker: BARLEY
text: That was amazing!

speaker: IAN
text: How long... was the rope gone?

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh, just like the second half of it.

speaker: IAN
text: I needed that rope!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh, but did you?

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ian! Look. Barley points to the top of the drawbridge structure.

speaker: BARLEY
text: It’s a raven. Barley takes out the wrinkled children’s menu.

speaker: BARLEY
text: The clue on the menu said Raven’s Point... Ian points to the mountains in the distance.

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah. In the mountains.

speaker: BARLEY
text: But maybe the puzzle didn’t mean the mountain. Maybe it means “follow where the raven is pointing!” Barley follows the line of the raven’s beak to a stone structure in the distance: Another raven.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ha-ha! It's another raven! That one could be pointing to another raven, all the way to the gem! Ian looks at the mountains, horrified.

speaker: IAN
text: I had us going the wrong way.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Well, I told ya. My gut knows where to go. Don’t ya, boy? Yes you do! Barley pats his gut and laughs proudly. Ian looks at Barley, impressed. Barley grabs Dad and heads to the van.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Come on, Dad! Guinevere will get us to that raven in no time. Suddenly, a POLICE SIREN. A police car crosses the bridge and screeches to a halt in front of them. Colt gets out of his car, struggling to pull his rear end out the door.

speaker: COLT
text: You guys are in trouble, big time!

speaker: BARLEY
text: No, no, no, Colt, we found a spell. If we finish it before sunset, we’ll get to see our father. Barley removes Dad’s torso. Colt looks confused.

speaker: COLT
text: Well, uh, your mom told me there was some kind of strange family issue going on and this is... definitely strange. But no, dang it. I’m not letting you upset your mother anymore! Now, you get in the vehicle, I’m escorting you home.

speaker: BARLEY
text: No, no way!

speaker: COLT
text: I’m giving you to the count of three. Colt stops his hoof once... then twice... then...

speaker: IAN
text: Okay. We’ll go. Ian grabs the van keys from Barley.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ian--

speaker: IAN
text: He’s a police officer. Ian turns and heads toward the driver’s side of the van.

speaker: BARLEY
text: What are you doing?

speaker: IAN
text: I don’t know. Ian HITS the gas.

speaker: COLT
text: Son of a-- Colt runs after the van. Exhausted, he talks into his radio.

speaker: COLT
text: I need backup. Runaway van!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ha-ha, YEAH! Iandore Lightfoot, breaking the rules! Ian keeps his eyes locked on the road, focused and frazzled.

speaker: IAN
text: I can’t believe I’m running from the cops!

speaker: BARLEY
text: You’re not running from the cops, you’re running from our mom’s boyfriend. Suddenly, a squadron of police cars come straight at them.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Okay, now you’re running from the cops. The cops approach from both directions. Ian whips Guinevere into a fishtail, drops the hammer, and evades the cops. Out the window, Ian spots the raven statue in the distance. He cranks the wheel toward it, down a narrow, unpaved road.

speaker: IAN
text: Hold on! Guinevere skids around an unbelievably tight turn at the edge of a cliff. Ian pats Guinevere’s dash board, encouragingly.

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah! Nice going, Guinevere! Woohoo! The road comes to a dead end. Ian turns the van around, but they see the cop cars coming up the narrow mountain pass. Ian gets out of the van.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh, what did I do?! I shouldn’t have driven away--

speaker: BARLEY
text: No, it was great! Barley points to boulders at the top of the narrow pass.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Hey! Block the road with those boulders!

speaker: IAN
text: What?! How?!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Arcane Lightning.

speaker: IAN
text: You said that’s the hardest spell.

speaker: BARLEY
text: You’re ready. Barley hands Ian the book and points to the spell.

speaker: BARLEY
text: “To make lightning strike with ease, one must follow all decrees.” You have to do everything. Speak from your heart's fire, trust yourself, focus, all of it! Ian picks up the staff and points it at the boulders.

speaker: IAN
text: Voltar Thundasir. A tiny bit of electricity glows in his staff, but quickly peters out. He tries again.

speaker: IAN
text: Voltar Thundasir. The staff glows again, but quickly fades. Ian points the staff at the boulders again.

speaker: IAN
text: Voltar Thundasir. The magic gives out.

speaker: IAN
text: I can’t, I can’t do it! Ian sighs and turns to Barley and Dad.

speaker: IAN
text: We’re not gonna see you, Dad. And it’s all my fault. Barley sees how upset Ian is. He turns, starts up Guinevere, and puts in a tape labelled RISE TO VALHALLA. Sad, heroic, music blares.

speaker: IAN
text: What are you doing? Barley aligns the steering wheel, puts a rock on the gas pedal, knocks the gear shift to “O.” Then watches her go. Her turn signal pops out and falls to the ground. The glove compartment pops open and parking violations fly out the windows. Barley salutes her. She rides up the edge of the narrow pass, SLAMS into the rocks, flips onto her back in the middle of the road, and is covered by boulders... completely blocking the road. The police can’t get through.

speaker: COLT
text: What the-- Barley picks up Guinevere’s turn signal light, gives it a loving rub, and puts it in his pocket.

speaker: IAN
text: Barley...

speaker: BARLEY
text: She was just a beat up old van. Come on, we gotta go. Barley ducks under the guard rail and walks through the brush with Dad. Ian climbs the guard rail and follows. We see the boys walk off toward the lowering sun.

speaker: IAN
text: Wait. Ian wipes dirt from the disk. They see the reflection of the raven’s beak pointing back at the chest of the raven statue. Ian goes to the statue, examines the chest, and pulls out a loose tile with a distinctive shape.

speaker: BARLEY
text: You see that, Dad? The apprentice has become the master. Barley takes a closer look at the tile. There’s a hieroglyphic engraved in it: wavy lines that lead to an "X".

speaker: BARLEY
text: It looks like... water. They look around and see a river flowing into a tunnel. They give each other an excited glance.

speaker: IAN
text: So what’s the X mean?

speaker: BARLEY
text: On a quest, an X only means one thing. We go to the end of the water, we’ll find that Phoenix Gem.

speaker: IAN
text: AH! Ugh!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ugh! They flap their horrible wings and flutter out of the tunnel.

speaker: IAN
text: Unicorns! Ugh! The boys and Dad climb deeper into the tunnel, along the river. Barley snacks on cheese puffs. Ian looks up to see ancient paintings adorning the walls.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Cool... Ian holds up the torch to try to see the end of the tunnel. It just disappears into endless darkness.

speaker: IAN
text: This water could go on for miles. We don't have that kind of time.

speaker: BARLEY
text: If we had something to float on we could cast a velocity spell on it. Fly down the tunnel like a magic jet ski. Ian looks around.

speaker: IAN
text: There’s not much to float on.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Remember, on a quest you have to use what you’ve got. Barley looks at the cheese puff he’s about to eat.

speaker: IAN
text: Magnora Gantuan! A FLASH. Then Ian, Barley, and Dad float into frame riding on a GIANT cheese puff. Ian wields the staff.

speaker: IAN
text: Accelior! The cheese puff races into the darkness of the tunnel.

speaker: IAN
text: This is actually kinda cool! So, what other spells do you know?

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh, brace yourself, young mage! I can show you all there is to know of magic!

speaker: LAUREL
text: Colt, we know where the boys are going, we just have to get to them before they unleash the curse!

speaker: COLT
text: The what?

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Let’s CRUSH some curses! She slams the energy drink and tosses it over her shoulder.

speaker: LAUREL
text: The curse! The curse that protects the gem by turning into a rock dragon or something--

speaker: COLT
text: A what dragon?!

speaker: LAUREL
text: I can’t explain it, I just know we have to get to the boys, fast! The Manticore cracks open another energy drink.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Oh, we’ll get them FAST, we’ll get to them SO FAST! Laurel takes the energy drink from the Manticore, shakes her head, and pours the rest out the window.

speaker: COLT
text: Well, I almost had them, but Ian, he just... drove off.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Huh? Good for him.

speaker: COLT
text: What?

speaker: LAUREL
text: No, I just mean... he’s scared to drive. Thinks something’s gonna come at him out of the-- A flying SPRITE hits the windshield.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Ahhh!

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Ahhh!

speaker: COLT
text: Ahhh!

speaker: WINDSHIELD SPRITE
text: Ahhh! Laurel can’t see. She turns on the windshield wipers in the chaos, then steers the car off the road, into a ditch. She and the Manticore get out of the car, dazed. We can hear Colt’s muffled voice inside.

speaker: COLT
text: Laurel, what happened?! What’s going on? The Sprite peels herself off of the windshield.

speaker: WINDSHIELD SPRITE
text: You almost killed me, lady!

speaker: LAUREL
text: Are you okay? Where did you come from? Suddenly, a swarm of sprites flies in.

speaker: DEWDROP
text: You were in our flight path!

speaker: FLYING SPRITES
text: Yeah! Come on! / Watch where you’re going! / Are you alright?

speaker: WINDSHIELD SPRITE
text: I’m fine. The Manticore looks at the demolished car.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Oh, no! Our transport! How are we going to get to your sons now? Laurel looks around for any sign of hope.

speaker: DEWDROP
text: Come on Pixie Dusters, lets take to the skies! Laurel watches the sprites fly away and gets an idea.

speaker: LAUREL
text: How do you feel about exercising those wings? The Manticore gives her wings a nervous flutter.

speaker: IAN
text: Boombastia! Just a small spark. Barley adjust Ian’s shoulder like he did with the growth spell.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Remember...

speaker: IAN
text: Ah, right. Boombastia! Beautiful fireworks explode from the staff.

speaker: IAN
text: Whoa!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Yeah! You’re a natural! Barley grabs a chunk of cheese doodle and THROWS it at Ian.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Think fast!

speaker: IAN
text: Aloft Elevar! Ian catches it with magic.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ha-ha! Yeah! He tosses it back to Barley who pops it in his mouth.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Nice! Barley eats another fist-sized chunk of the cheese doodle.

speaker: IAN
text: Careful how much boat you’re eating there, man, we still gotta make it to the end of the tunnel.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Good point.

speaker: IAN
text: I can't believe I'm this close to actually talking to Dad.

speaker: BARLEY
text: You know what I’m gonna ask him? If he ever gave himself a wizard name.

speaker: IAN
text: What?

speaker: BARLEY
text: Well, ‘cause he was into magic. Lots of wizards have cool names: Alora the Majestic, Birdar the Fanciful. Anyway, it’ll just be nice to have more than four memories of him.

speaker: IAN
text: Three.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Hmm?

speaker: IAN
text: You only have three memories.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh. Yeah.

speaker: IAN
text: Barley, do you have another memory of Dad you haven't told me? Barley pauses, a little pained.

speaker: BARLEY
text: It’s... it’s just not my favorite.

speaker: IAN
text: What do you mean? Barley gives Ian a side glance, hesitant to start.

speaker: BARLEY
text: When Dad was sick... I was supposed to go in and say goodbye to him. But he was hooked up to all these tubes and... he just didn’t look like himself. I got scared. And I didn’t go in. But that’s when I decided, I was never gonna be scared ever again. Ian smiles, bittersweet. Barley looks ahead and points.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ah, looks like we’re coming up on something. The walls of the cavern grow ornate, wizard statues guard the tunnel. The boat slows and stops at the edge of a landing. The boys and Dad approach an ancient, ornate archway.

speaker: BARLEY
text: The final gauntlet. The Phoenix Gem is just on the other side.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Careful, there could be booby traps.

speaker: IAN
text: This place is, like, a thousand years old. There’s no way there could be– A blade swings from the wall, cutting off Dad’s torso and flinging it behind them. A gust blows out Barley’s torch. Behind them, a trap door in the ceiling slowly opens, casting a sickly green light into the tunnel.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh no, it can’t be... A giant GELATINOUS CUBE filled with skeletons drops from a hole in the ceiling, closing them off from the way they came.

speaker: BARLEY
text: A GELATINOUS CUBE! The cube moves toward them. As it does, it runs over Dad’s dismembered torso. The torso immediately dissolves. They look to the other end of the tunnel and see a door slowly lowering from above, about to close them off inside the chamber with the gelatinous cube.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Run! Ian sees there are shapes and symbols on the path stones.

speaker: IAN
text: Whoa, wait! It’s some kind of puzzle. We gotta figure it out before--

speaker: BARLEY
text: No time. Grab a shield. Barley and Ian grab shields from skeletons and RUN. As they step on path stones, arrows release from the walls and strike the shields. At the other side, they STOP in front of a wide chasm with spikes at the bottom.

speaker: IAN
text: Jump!

speaker: BARLEY
text: What?

speaker: IAN
text: Trust me. Barley jumps and begins to fall toward the spikes.

speaker: IAN
text: Aloft Elevar! Ian catches Barley in mid-air with a levitation spell.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ha-ha! Ian and Dad leap off the edge, using Barley as a stepping stone to bound to the other side.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ow! Hey! Ian lands on the other side and looks back.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ian... The cube is closing in on Barley, who floats over the chasm. Ian uses magic to lift Barley to safety, just barely sliding him up between the gelatinous cube and the ledge. Ian and Barley slide under the closing door, then notice Dad is still on the other side. Ian grabs his leash and tugs him under the door just as it closes. Ian stands and starts to move forward, but Barley stops him.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Whoa whoa whoa! Barley points at the floor. There’s a star-shaped tile on the floor, similar to the ones in the gauntlet.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Don’t step on that. There’s a clanging noise, slow and deep. Suddenly water RUSHES into the silo from holes at the bottom. It quickly lifts them off their feet, toward the ceiling.

speaker: IAN
text: I didn’t touch it! Barley holds onto Dad, trying to keep him above water with them. Ian aims the staff at a hole at the top of the silo.

speaker: IAN
text: Voltar Thundasir! A lightning bolt shoots forth, bouncing off the door. As they get closer to the top, they can see the hole the light is coming through is star-shaped. Ian sticks his head underwater and looks at star-shaped stone on the floor.

speaker: IAN
text: The tile down there has the same shape as the opening!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Maybe we were supposed to step on it?

speaker: IAN
text: What?!

speaker: BARLEY
text: I got it!

speaker: IAN
text: No, Barley! Barley swims to the bottom of the silo. He STANDS on the stone and the door above them begins to open.

speaker: IAN
text: It’s working! But Barley can’t hold his breath long enough. He swims back to the surface and the door quickly closes

speaker: BARLEY
text: It's impossible! No one can hold their breath that long! Ian and Barley look at each other, then at Dad. Ian lets go of Dad, letting him sink to the bottom. Ian guides Dad toward the stone with his leash, but Dad keeps missing. The water fills to the top. The boys take a deep breath then are fully submerged. Ian finally guides Dad to the stone. The door above them opens as the stone finally LOCKS into place. The boys GASP to the surface. They climb out of the water and reel in Dad with the leash.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ha-ha! We made it! Just above them is a circular portal.

speaker: BARLEY
text: The Phoenix Gem awaits beyond this door! Shall we?

speaker: IAN
text: We certainly shall! They climb the ladder, laughing as they clumsily help Dad.

speaker: IAN
text: Dad, we have followed the quest and it has led us to our victory! Ian slides the portal aside and sunlight seeps in...

speaker: IAN
text: We’re back home.

speaker: BARLEY
text: How did we--? No, that doesn't make sense! We took the Path of Peril. We followed the ravens, we went to the end of the water... Barley looks more closely at the tile.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Unless the X meant stay away from the water. He turns the tile so the X is on the bottom.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Or it could be, like, a campfire... It’s okay. We can figure this out.

speaker: IAN
text: Figure out what? We’re back where we started!

speaker: BARLEY
text: I mean, it has to be here, there was a gauntlet. I mean, unless, that gauntlet was for, coincidentally, some different quest. That's a possibility.

speaker: IAN
text: What?!

speaker: BARLEY
text: No, no, no, this has to be where the Phoenix Gem is. I followed my gut.

speaker: IAN
text: Oh no...

speaker: BARLEY
text: What?

speaker: IAN
text: The gem is in the mountain. The mountain we could have been to hours ago if we’d just... If we’d just stayed on the expressway.

speaker: BARLEY
text: No, the expressway is too obvious, remember?! You can never take the--

speaker: IAN
text: If I hadn’t listened to you, Okay?! I can't believe this. You act like you know what you’re doing, but you don’t have a clue... and that’s because you are a screw-up! And now you have screwed up my chance to have the ONE thing I never had! Barley looks at Ian, hurt. Ian takes Dad by the leash walks into the park.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Where are you going?

speaker: IAN
text: To spend what little time we have left with Dad! Ian looks at the staff in disgust. He hands it to Barley.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ian, wait! We can still find the Phoenix Gem! We just have to keep looking! But Ian just keeps walking with Dad further into the park.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ian! IAN!

speaker: IAN
text: No, Dad. He’s not here. Ian and Dad sit down on the ground, facing the setting sun.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Come on, where is it? Barley looks at the fountain.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Follow the water! Barley races to the fountain and sloshes in the dirty water looking for a clue. Two nearby construction workers notice. CONSTRUCTION WORKER #2 Alright, come on, out of the fountain.

speaker: BARLEY
text: No! I’m looking for an ancient gem!

speaker: FENNWICK
text: Yeah, we know the old days.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Stop! Please! No! The construction workers pull him away from the fountain.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Okay! Okay! Ow! Okay, I'm leaving! Barley breaks free from them and races back to the fountain.

speaker: FENNWICK
text: Hey! CONSTRUCTION WORKER #2 Hey! Oh, come on. They try to grab him again, but he awkwardly shimmies to the top, arms wrapped around the spire, wet and pathetic. CONSTRUCTION WORKER #2 Can someone call the cops?! We got the history buff again! Barley looks desperate and confused. The screwup everyone always believed him to be.

speaker: IAN
text: I can’t do this!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Yes, you can!

speaker: IAN
text: I’m not ready!

speaker: BARLEY
text: You’ll never be ready! MERGE!!

speaker: IAN
text: AHHH!!! Ian floors it and drives onto the freeway.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ha-ha! Nice job!

speaker: BARLEY
text: THINK FAST!

speaker: IAN
text: Aloft Elevar! Ian catches it with magic.

speaker: YOUNG BARLEY
text: Woohoo!

speaker: BARLEY
text: I know you’re stronger than that!

speaker: BARLEY
text: My little brother has the magic gift!

speaker: BARLEY
text: DON’T HOLD BACK!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Hey, you can do this.

speaker: OFFICER SPECTER
text: Okay, come on. Get down right now. Barley notices a shape on the fountain that matches the tile from the entrance to the tunnel. His eyes light up. He slowly pushes the tile into the matching shape in the fountain. The tile slides in with a CLACK. It’s a perfect fit. An eye atop the fountain opens to reveal the PHOENIX GEM.

speaker: BARLEY
text: YEAH! YEAH! He grabs the gem, but doesn’t notice a red mist that pours from the fountain. Ian clears the woods and sees Barley on the fountain, holding the gem triumphantly.

speaker: IAN
text: BARLEY! Barley looks at Ian.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Ian, look! Ian smiles, impressed. But then sees the ominous red smoke.

speaker: IAN
text: Behind you! Barley turns to see the red smoke go past the police officers. They slowly back into the crowd.

speaker: BARLEY
text: It’s a curse! The smoke pulls apart the high school with its red coils,forming the shape of a massive DRAGON. It turns to reveal it has the eerie, smiling face of the school’s dragon mascot. The dragon sees the gem in Barley’s hand and ROARS a terrifying, garbled version of the school bell.

speaker: IAN
text: Barley, RUN! Barley jumps down from the fountain, grabs the staff, and takes off running. He calls out to the dragon.

speaker: BARLEY
text: What do you want?! The gem?! Fine. Take it! He THROWS the gem as far as he can in the other direction. The dragon turns to chase the gem. But when it lands, we see it isn’t the gem at all. It’s Guinevere’s turn signal. Barley and Ian race toward each other. Barley holds up his hand to reveal he still has the real gem. The dragon turns, sees Barley with the gem, and breathes a wall of fire between the boys. Ian and Barley are thrown to the ground, they can’t get to each other. The dragon takes a flying leap toward the boys and Dad. Suddenly, a dark shape sweeps down from the sky. The Manticore, holding the Curse Crusher sword, BASHES the dragon to the ground.

speaker: BARLEY
text: The Manticore! Ian squints and sees someone riding atop the Manticore.

speaker: IAN
text: Mom?! Laurel stands atop the Manticore heroically.

speaker: LAUREL
text: It’s okay, boys! We’ll take care ofThe Manticore tips to one side.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Whoa, you’re tilting, you're tilting! The Manticore straightens.

speaker: IAN
text: Mom!

speaker: LAUREL
text: Go see your father!

speaker: BARLEY
text: It’s okay! If they stab the beast’s core with that sword, the curse will be broken! Come on! The Manticore flies toward the dragon, taunting it into a chase. As the dragon follows briefly distracted, the Manticore loops around and cuts off the dragon’s wings. The boys climb the hill to get around the fire: Ian up one side with Dad, Barley up the other with the staff and gem. As they meet at the top, Barley hands Ian the staff.

speaker: IAN
text: Barley, what I said before-- I am so sorry--

speaker: BARLEY
text: There’s no time! The sun is about to set! Ian holds out the staff with the gem inside.

speaker: IAN
text: Only once is all we get, Grant me this rebirth, 'Til tomorrow’s sun has set, One day to walk the earth! A beam of light shoots from the staff. Ian holds on strong. The Manticore flies back toward the dragon, fast and fierce.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Time to crush a curse! But the dragon swats the Manticore out of the sky with its tail. Laurel and the Manticore CRASH to the ground. Laurel gets up, but the Manticore cannot.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Ooh, my back! A shadow passes over them as the dragon makes its way toward the boys. Laurel lifts the heavy Curse Crusher and wields it.

speaker: LAUREL
text: I am a mighty warrior... Laurel clambers up the tail of the dragon with the sword. The music from Laurel’s warrior workout video plays. Laurel runs to the curse’s core, glowing deep beneath its armor. Laurel holds the sword above her head with both hands and STABS the sword into the core.

speaker: LAUREL
text: I AM A MIGHTY WARRIOR! The dragon slows to a stop. The boys watch the red of the curse turn blue. Laurel holds the sword with great effort.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Hurry, I can’t hold this for long! The sword is up to the hilt in the armor, but just barely piercing the red core. Meanwhile, the gem lifts out of the staff and spins. Red, ominous light grows around it, the staff begins to shake.

speaker: IAN
text: No, no, no, no, no-- The red magic swells and is about to explode...

speaker: IAN
text: Barley! Barley runs and braces his back against Ian’s. There’s an explosion. Then the gem floats over Dad and begins raining magic down upon him, slowly building the rest of him. The boys watch with joy. The dragon’s armor begins to shift, slowly working the sword out of the core. Now freed, the dragon knocks Laurel off its back. The sword falls deep into a pile of lockers.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Boys! It’s coming back! The dragon’s eyes turn immediately to the gem. Ian and Barley turn to the dragon as Dad continues to generate. The sun is beginning to set.

speaker: BARLEY
text: I’ll go distract it!

speaker: IAN
text: What? No! If you do that, you’ll miss Dad.

speaker: BARLEY
text: It’s okay. Say hi to Dad for me. Ian takes this in and suddenly realizes what he has to do.

speaker: IAN
text: No. You go and say goodbye.

speaker: BARLEY
text: What?

speaker: IAN
text: I had someone who looked out for me, someone who pushed me to be more than I ever thought I could be. I never had a dad... but I always had you. Barley opens his mouth to respond, but before he can say anything, Ian takes the staff and races off. Ian leaps off the ledge, calling out the trust bridge spell.

speaker: IAN
text: Bridrigar Invisia! He lands on the bridge midair and runs toward the dragon.

speaker: IAN
text: Boombastia! Fireworks shoot from his staff, blinding the dragon. The dragon whips its tail toward Ian. It misses the first time, but then the dragon lifts its tail to strike Ian again.

speaker: IAN
text: Aloft Elevar! Ian stops the tail by levitation. He strains to hold it. Meanwhile, Laurel tries desperately to reach the hilt of the Curse Crusher deep in the locker pile. The dragon whips its tail, sending Ian and the staff soaring. Ian crashes to the ground, watching helplessly as the staff splashes into the ocean.

speaker: IAN
text: NO! The dragon marches straight towards Barley and Dad.

speaker: IAN
text: No. No. No-- No! Laurel gets her fingertips on the sword. It’s precariously perched, slipping toward a deeper fall. Ian searches for a way.

speaker: IAN
text: Use what I have. Uh, what do I have? I have nothing! He looks at his empty hands.

speaker: IAN
text: Splinter... Ian sees a splinter from the staff in his hand.

speaker: IAN
text: Magic in every fiber. Ian bites out the splinter and holds it out in his hand.

speaker: IAN
text: Magnora Gantuan! The splinter grows into a full-size staff. Ian sees the beast closing in on Barley and Dad.

speaker: IAN
text: NO! Ian points the staff and yells...

speaker: IAN
text: Voltar Thundasir! A colossal bolt of lighting shoots from his staff and blasts most of the armor off the dragon. It keeps advancing, still dragon-shaped, but with its core clearly exposed. The Curse Crusher begins to fall, but Laurel JUST grabs it by the tip of its blade. She stands and calls out to Ian.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Ian! She hurls the sword toward Ian with all her might.

speaker: IAN
text: Accelior! Ian rushes the sword mid-air with the velocity spell and plunges it straight into the core of the curse. It billows into the air as a red cloud, then dissipates into nothing. Ian falls to the ground, spent. The staff clatters away from him and is blocked by the rubble that was once the dragon. Ian gets up but is stopped by a sharp pain in his ankle.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Ian!? Ian turns to see Laurel through a crack in the debris. Colt runs up alongside her. Suddenly there's a warm burst of light from atop the peak. Laurel and Colt look toward the light, stunned. Ian climbs the rubble to find a gap through which he can see. Barley is face to face with a glowing figure. The glowing subsides to reveal Dad. All of him. Barley and Dad look uncomfortable at first; two strangers getting to know each other. Ian can only see Dad’s back. Ian slips from his position and loses sight of Barley and Dad. He turns to see the sun is disappearing. He climbs back up and continues to watch. Dad and Barley talk and laugh together. Dad reaches out and gives his big son a hug. The sun sets. And Dad vanishes in Barley's arms. Barley collects himself, then climbs down the peak. Tears fill Laurel’s eyes, she leans on Colt. Barley reaches down to help up Ian. The boys stand atop the rubble face to face.

speaker: IAN
text: What did he say?

speaker: BARLEY
text: He said he always thought his wizard name would be Wilden the Whimsical.

speaker: IAN
text: Wow, that’s really terrible.

speaker: BARLEY
text: I know. He also said he’s very proud of the person you grew up to be.

speaker: IAN
text: Well, I owe an awful lot of that to you.

speaker: BARLEY
text: He kinda said that too. Oh, and he told me to give you this. Barley leans down and gives Ian a big, loving hug. Ian smiles, and hugs him back.

speaker: IAN
text: Long ago, the world was full of wonder! Over the rolling hills, into suburban neighborhoods.

speaker: IAN
text: It was adventurous, exciting, and best of all, there was magic. A familiar building at the top of a hill - the Manticore’s Tavern with a sign that reads “GRAND RE-OPENING.”

speaker: BULLSEYE SPRITE
text: Ha-ha! Bullseye! The Manticore, her wings outstretched, tells a tale.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: And then with a slash of my mighty sword, I severed the beast’s wings from its wretched body! She blows a flame to light the candles on a birthday cake. The kids’ birthday hats get a little scorched.

speaker: MANTICORE
text: Okay, who wants cake? She proudly lifts the glorious Curse Crusher to the skies, then brings it down hard and slices the cake. Frosting splatters on the kids faces. There’s a beat, then...

speaker: BIRTHDAY KIDS
text: YEAH!!! The parents watch uncomfortably. Cake on their faces as well.

speaker: IAN
text: And I think with a little bit of magic in your life... you can do almost anything. A student, YELDAEH, raises her hand.

speaker: YELDAEH
text: Is that how you put the school back together?

speaker: IAN
text: Uh, yes! Another student, THEYUS, interrupts.

speaker: THEYUS
text: Is that also how you destroyed the school in the first place?

speaker: IAN
text: Uh... Also yes? The bell RINGS. The students leave. Sadalia and her group of friends walk up to Ian.

speaker: SADALIA
text: Hey, that was great.

speaker: IAN
text: Thanks!

speaker: GURGE
text: You going to the park later?

speaker: IAN
text: Yeah! See you there! They all wave goodbye to Ian as they leave.

speaker: IAN
text: Mom! Blazey barrels around the corner and knocks Ian to the ground. But Ian stays there and wrestles playfully with her. Laurel hustles around the corner with the spray bottle.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Blazey! Down!

speaker: IAN
text: Who’s a good dragon? Who’s a good dragon?

speaker: LAUREL
text: So, how was school?

speaker: IAN
text: It was... really good.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Well, alright. Colt sidles up to Laurel.

speaker: COLT
text: Hey! There he is. You working hard?

speaker: IAN
text: Nope, hardly workin'. Colt throws his head back and laughs. Laurel gets a text.

speaker: LAUREL
text: Oh, I gotta go. I’m meeting the Manticore for a night out. Laurel picks up a battle axe.

speaker: OFFICER GORE
text: We have a 113 in progress. All units report. Colt kisses Laurel, then he leaves.

speaker: COLT
text: Duty calls. Time to hit the trail!

speaker: LAUREL
text: You forgot your keys!

speaker: COLT
text: Don’t need ‘em! I was born to run. H’yah! Colt runs full-speed down the road, his hair flowing.

speaker: IAN
text: So, how’s the new van?

speaker: BARLEY
text: Oh, Guinevere the Second is great. I’ve almost got enough saved up for a sweet paint job.

speaker: IAN
text: No... please don’t.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Why not?

speaker: IAN
text: Because I already took care of it. The new van has a Pegasus with a warrior Barley on her back and a wizard Ian riding behind him.

speaker: BARLEY
text: YEAH!

speaker: BARLEY
text: Okay, best way to the park is to take a little something called the Road of Ruin.

speaker: IAN
text: Mmm... Too obvious.

speaker: BARLEY
text: Wait, what?

speaker: IAN
text: On a quest, the clear path is never the right one. The van lifts into the air and flies into the horizon.

speaker: BARLEY
text: YEAH!!
